items.hero_tagline: Industry-standard BIM for AEC — compared honestly against MIT open-core.

features.name: Parametric family editor (nested families, type catalogue)
Sheets and title blocks (multi-sheet drawing sets)
Steel column base plate design (AISC DG-1 §3.1 + AISC 360-22 §J8, concentric axial only)
features.kerf: evidence: "packages/kerf-cad-core/src/kerf_cad_core/arch/base_plate_aisc.py"; note: "AISC DG-1 2nd ed. §3.1 + AISC 360-22 §J8: bearing Pp=0.85·f'c·A1·√(A2/A1) A2/A1≤4 φ_c=0.65; plate sized to φ_c·Pp≥P_u and cover 0.95·d×0.80·bf; m=(N−0.95·d)/2 (Eq 3.1-1); n=(B−0.80·bf)/2 (Eq 3.1-2); n'=√(d·bf)/4; X=[4·d·bf/(d+bf)²]·[P_u/(φ_c·Pp)] (Eq 3.1-8); λ=min(1,2·√X/(1+√(1−X))); t=l·√(2·P_u/(0.9·Fy·B·N)) Murray-Stockwell (Eq 3.1-5); W14x90 P=1000kN f'c=30MPa ped600×600→plate 295×340×15mm DCR=0.318; arch_design_base_plate LLM tool; SCOPE: concentric axial ONLY — moment (DG-1 §3.2), anchor rods (§3.3–3.4), shear lug (§3.5) NOT modelled"

features.name: Column axial-load check (AISC 360-22 §E3 + ACI 318-19 §22.4)
features.kerf: evidence: "packages/kerf-cad-core/src/kerf_cad_core/arch/column_load_check.py"; note: "AISC 360-22 §E3 inelastic/elastic Euler buckling (E3-2/E3-3) + ACI 318-19 §22.4.2.2 short tied/spiral column; phi_Pn, DCR, governing mode, KL/r>200 slender flag; arch_check_column_load LLM tool"

features.name: Beam deflection & moment (SS / cantilever / fixed-fixed, Roark 9e §8)
features.kerf: evidence: "packages/kerf-cad-core/src/kerf_cad_core/arch/beam_deflection.py"; note: "Euler-Bernoulli closed-form for 5 cases: SS+point (Roark 8.1 case 7), SS+UDL (case 2), cantilever+point (case 1), cantilever+UDL (case 3), fixed-fixed+UDL (case 15); delta_max, M_max, V_max, deflection location; arch_compute_beam_deflection LLM tool; SCOPE: linear-elastic only — no buckling, no yield, no shear deformation"

features.name: Shallow footing bearing capacity (Meyerhof 1963 general eq.)
features.kerf: evidence: "packages/kerf-cad-core/src/kerf_cad_core/arch/footing_bearing.py"; note: "Meyerhof (1963) general bearing capacity: q_ult = c·Nc·sc·dc + γ·Df·Nq·sq·dq + 0.5·γ·B·Nγ·sγ·dγ; N-factors (Bowles 5e §4 / Das 8e §3); shape+depth factors for strip/square/circular/rectangular; φ=0 Prandtl limit Nc=5.14; arch_compute_bearing_capacity LLM tool; SCOPE: Meyerhof shape+depth only — no Brinch Hansen Ir correction, no inclined/eccentric loads, no seismic, no liquefaction"

features.name: Two-way slab deflection (Kirchhoff plate, Timoshenko §44 Tables 41–42)
features.kerf: evidence: "packages/kerf-cad-core/src/kerf_cad_core/arch/slab_deflection.py"; note: "Kirchhoff thin-plate δ=α·q·a⁴/D; D=E·h³/(12·(1-ν²)); α from Timoshenko Table 41 (SS, interpolated at any b/a) and Table 42 (fixed, a/b=1 exact); SS: a/b=1→α=0.00406, a/b=1.5→α=0.00772, a/b=2→α=0.01013, ∞→0.01302 strip limit; FF: a/b=1→α=0.00126; moments M_xx=β_x·q·a², M_yy=β_y·q·a²; arch_compute_slab_deflection LLM tool; SCOPE: linear-elastic Kirchhoff only — no Mindlin shear deformation, no cracking, no plastic hinges, no creep/shrinkage"

features.name: ASCE 7-22 wind loads — MWFRS Directional Procedure (§26–27)
features.kerf: evidence: "packages/kerf-cad-core/src/kerf_cad_core/arch/wind_load_asce7.py"; note: "ASCE 7-22 §26–27 Directional Procedure: qz=0.00256·Kz·Kzt·Kd·V² (psf); Kz per Table 26.10-1 (Exp B/C/D, floor at 15 ft); Cp_windward=+0.8, Cp_leeward per Fig 27.4-1 (−0.5/−0.3/−0.2 interpolated by L/B); G=0.85 rigid; design pressure p=qz·G·Cp; arch_compute_wind_load LLM tool; SCOPE: Directional Procedure §27 only — Envelope Procedure §28 not implemented, tornado loads §32 not implemented, internal pressure GCpi §26.13 excluded, parapet+roof pressures excluded, flexible-building dynamic G §26.11.2 not supported"

features.name: ASCE 7-22 wind loads — Components and Cladding §30.3 (windows, doors, roof panels)
features.kerf: evidence: "packages/kerf-cad-core/src/kerf_cad_core/arch/wind_component_cladding.py"; note: "ASCE 7-22 §30.3 C&C: qh=0.00256·Kh·Kzt·Kd·V² (psf); six zones: Zone_1_interior_wall(+0.9/−1.1 @ 10 ft²), Zone_4_wall_edge(+1.0/−1.1), Zone_5_corner_wall(+1.0/−1.4), Zone_1_roof_interior(+0.3/−1.0 @ 10 ft²), Zone_2_roof_edge(+0.3/−1.8), Zone_3_roof_corner(+0.3/−2.8); log-linear effective-area reduction [10→500 ft² walls; 10→100 ft² roofs]; GCpi=±0.18 enclosed (Table 26.13-1): p_pos=qh·(GCp+0.18), p_neg=qh·(GCp−0.18); arch_compute_wind_cc_pressure LLM tool; SCOPE: enclosed buildings only — partially-enclosed GCpi=±0.55 NOT modelled; h ≤ 60 ft (§30.3 Low-Rise only); roof slopes ≤7° only; parapets §30.9 not implemented; min pressure ±16 psf (§30.2.2) not auto-enforced"

features.name: Lateral-torsional buckling check (AISC 360-22 §F2, compact I-shapes)
features.kerf: evidence: "packages/kerf-cad-core/src/kerf_cad_core/arch/lateral_bracing_check.py"; note: "AISC 360-22 §F2 LRFD: Lp=1.76·ry·√(E/Fy) (F2-5); Lr via F2-6; Zone 1 Lb≤Lp→yielding Mn=Mp; Zone 2 inelastic LTB Mn=Cb·[Mp−(Mp−0.7·Fy·Sx)·ramp]≤Mp (F2-2); Zone 3 elastic LTB Mn=Fcr·Sx≤Mp (F2-3/4); φ_b=0.90; c=1.0 (F2-8a); arch_check_lateral_bracing LLM tool; W14x90 oracle: Lp≈3983mm, Lr≈12962mm, Mp≈887.6kN·m; SCOPE: doubly symmetric compact I-shapes only — channels, tees, built-up sections out of scope; Cb must be supplied by caller"

features.name: Two-way punching shear check (ACI 318-19 §22.6, no shear reinforcement)
features.kerf: evidence: "packages/kerf-cad-core/src/kerf_cad_core/arch/punching_shear.py"; note: "ACI 318-19 §22.6 + Wight §13.10: b_0 at d/2 from column face; vc=min(0.33·λ·√f'c, 0.17·(1+2/β_c)·λ·√f'c, 0.083·(α_s·d/b_0+2)·λ·√f'c); φ·Vn=φ·vc·b_0·d (φ=0.75); square/rectangular/circular columns; oracle 300mm sq col d=160mm f'c=30MPa→b_0=1840mm→φ·Vn≈399kN; DCR for V=300/500/900kN; β_c>2 rectangular→aspect-ratio governs; arch_check_punching_shear LLM tool; SCOPE: Vs=0 (no shear reinforcement); no unbalanced moment; no openings/drop panels; no axial load effect; √f'c cap not auto-enforced"

features.name: RC shear wall out-of-plane flexural + slenderness check (ACI 318-19 §11.7)
features.kerf: evidence: "packages/kerf-cad-core/src/kerf_cad_core/arch/shear_wall_oop.py"; note: "ACI 318-19 §11.5.3 h/t≤30 slenderness; §11.7.5.1 empirical axial φPn=0.55·φ·f'c·Ag·[1−(k·h/(32·t))²] (k=0.8 fixed-fixed); §22.3 OOP unit-strip flexure a=(As_total·fy)/(0.85·f'c·1000); φMn=φ·As_total·fy·(d−a/2); Bresler linear DCR=Pu/φPn+Mu/φMn; oracle t=200mm h=3000mm f'c=30MPa fy=420MPa As_each_face=300mm²/m→φPn=1843kN/m φMn=27.1kNm/m DCR=0.791 (adequate); h/t>30→slenderness fail; Mu=40kNm/m→DCR=1.53 (FAIL); arch_check_shear_wall_oop LLM tool; SCOPE: empirical §11.7.5 only — slender-wall moment-magnifier (§11.8/§6.7.3) NOT implemented; no P-delta; no biaxial bending; no in-plane shear (§11.6/§18.10); Bresler linear interaction only (not full PM curve); Wight §13.13; 34 tests pass"

features.name: Horizontal diaphragm in-plane shear capacity (AWC SDPWS-2021 §4.2 / SDI DDM04)
features.kerf: evidence: "packages/kerf-cad-core/src/kerf_cad_core/arch/diaphragm_shear.py"; note: "AWC SDPWS-2021 §4.2 wood structural panel + SDI DDM04 metal deck in-plane shear (ASD basis); v=V/L (plf); v_allow from SDPWS Table 4.2A (plywood 15/32": 6"oc→510 plf, 4"oc→665 plf, 2.5"oc→870 plf, 2"oc→1000 plf; plywood 19/32": 6"oc→640 plf; OSB 15/32"=plywood); species factor C_s (DF_L/SP=1.00, HF=0.90, SPF=0.80); unblocked×0.50 (§4.2.7 Case 1); linear nail-spacing interpolation; metal deck 22ga→480 plf, 18ga→760 plf (SDI DDM04 36/6 ASD); AR≤4:1 wood (SDPWS Table 4.2.4), ≤2:1 steel deck (IBC §2209.2); oracle 30×40ft plywood 15/32" blocked 6"oc DF_L V=20000lbs→v=667 plf>510→DCR=1.307 (FAIL); arch_check_diaphragm_shear LLM tool; SCOPE: in-plane unit shear ONLY — chord forces NOT computed; deflection NOT computed (use SDPWS Eq.4.2-1 separately); ASD only; SDC A–C only without §4.2.4 review; metal deck 36/6 weld pattern only; 41 tests pass"

features.name: Cantilever retaining wall stability (Rankine active / Bowles 5e §12.3)
features.kerf: evidence: "packages/kerf-cad-core/src/kerf_cad_core/arch/retaining_wall_stability.py"; note: "Rankine active Ka=tan²(45-φ/2); Pa=0.5·γ_s·H²·Ka at H/3; FoS_overturning=ΣM_resist/ΣM_overt (min 2.0); FoS_sliding=ΣW·tan(δ)/Pa (min 1.5); bearing q_max=(ΣW/B)·(1+6e/B) FoS_bearing=q_a/q_max (min 3.0); oracle H=3m B=2m φ=30°→Ka=1/3 Pa=27kN/m FoS_overt=4.16; arch_check_retaining_wall_stability LLM tool; SCOPE: Rankine active only (level cohesionless backfill) — NO surcharge; NO seismic (Mononobe-Okabe); NO passive resistance at toe; NO hydrostatic pressure; 27 tests pass"

features.name: Masonry/RC pier axial capacity with h/r slenderness (TMS 402-22 §8.3 + ACI 318-19 §22.4.2.2)
features.kerf: evidence: "packages/kerf-cad-core/src/kerf_cad_core/arch/pier_axial_capacity.py"; note: "TMS 402-22 §8.3 masonry: φ·Pn=φ·0.80·f'm·Ag·C_s; ACI 318-19 §22.4.2.2 RC: φ·Pn=φ·0.80·[0.85·f'c·(Ag-As)+fy·As]·C_s; slenderness factor C_s=1−(h_eff/(140·r))² (TMS Eq 8-22) valid h_eff/r≤99; r=min(width,thickness)/√12; k: fixed_fixed=0.5, pin_pin=1.0, fixed_pin=0.7, cantilever=2.0 (Drysdale-Hamid §10); oracle 200×200mm pin-pin h=3000mm clay_masonry f'm=10MPa→r=57.7mm h/r=52.0 C_s≈0.862 φ·Pn≈179.5kN; h/r>99→slenderness_limit_exceeded φ·Pn=0; arch_check_pier_axial LLM tool; SCOPE: concentric axial ONLY — no eccentricity, no moment interaction, no PM curve; TMS Eq 8-22 h_eff/r≤99 limit; grout/inspection Φ not applied; 49 tests pass"

features.name: Steel/RC/RM lintel design over wall opening (AISC Table 3-23 + ACI 318-19 §9 + TMS 402-22 §5)
features.kerf: evidence: "packages/kerf-cad-core/src/kerf_cad_core/arch/lintel_design.py"; note: "AISC Table 3-23 + ACI 318-19 §9 + TMS 402-22 §5; materials: steel/reinforced_concrete/reinforced_masonry; load model: w_u=1.2·DL+1.6·LL (ASCE 7-16 §2.3.1 combo 2) + masonry arching action (45° triangle when h_masonry≥L/2, TMS 402-22 Commentary §5.3.1; BIA TN-31B); steel: φ·Mn=0.90·Fy·S_x (rectangle approx), φ·Vn=1.00·0.6·Fy·0.5·b·h; RC ACI §9: ρ_max=0.018, φ·Mn=0.90·As·fy·(d−a/2), φ·Vn=0.75·0.17·√f'c·b·d (V_c only); RM TMS §9.3: ρ_max=0.010, φ·Mn=0.90·As·fy·(d−a/2), φ·Vn=0.80·A_n·√f'm/3; deflection L/240 (wall/roof) or L/360 (floor); oracle steel L=1200mm 101.6×101.6mm Fy=250MPa DL=5 LL=3: M_factored=1.944kN·m, service M=1.44kN·m, φ·Mn=39.33kN·m, adequate=True; arch_design_lintel LLM tool; SCOPE: simple span ONLY — continuous-beam moment redistribution NOT modelled; steel: rectangle approx (actual W/L-shapes have higher S_x/I_x); LTB not checked; RC: ρ_max estimate, V_c only (no stirrups); RM: ρ_max estimate, no shear rebar; combo 1.2D+1.6L only; 53 tests pass"

features.name: Cast-in-place headed anchor bolt tensile pullout (ACI 318-19 §17.6 + ACI 355.2)
features.kerf: evidence: "packages/kerf-cad-core/src/kerf_cad_core/arch/anchor_bolt_pullout.py"; note: "ACI 318-19 §17.6 + ACI 355.2: three limit states — steel §17.6.1: A_se=0.85·π·d²/4, φ·N_sa=φ_s·A_se·fy (φ_s=0.75); concrete breakout §17.6.2: N_b=k_c_SI·λ·√f'c·hef^1.5 (k_c_SI=2.40 cracked/3.40 uncracked, SI equiv. of ACI imperial k_c=24/34; N,MPa,mm), A_Nco=9·hef², A_Nc from projected-cone geometry clipped at edges, ψ_ed=1.0 if c_a,min≥1.5·hef else 0.7+0.3·c_a,min/(1.5·hef) (§17.6.2.4.1), ψ_c=1.0 cracked/1.25 uncracked, φ·N_cb=φ_c·(A_Nc/A_Nco)·ψ_ed·ψ_c·N_b; pullout §17.6.3: N_p=8·A_brg·f'c, φ·N_pn=φ_c·N_p; governing=min(φ·N_sa,φ·N_cb,φ·N_pn); oracle 16mm bolt hef=200mm edge=300mm fc=25MPa fy=420MPa A_brg=400mm² cracked: N_b=33941N, φ·N_cb=22.06kN governs, N=15kN→adequate, N=50kN→FAILS; arch_check_anchor_pullout LLM tool; SCOPE: tension-only (no shear §17.7); cracked concrete default (k_c=2.40, ψ_c=1.0); λ=1.0 NW concrete; ψ_ec=1.0 concentric; splitting/side-face-blowout/adhesive NOT checked; one close edge assumed; 52 tests pass"

features.name: Plain/masonry bearing wall axial capacity + DCR (ACI 318-19 §11.5.3.1 + TMS 402-22 §8.3)
features.kerf: evidence: "packages/kerf-cad-core/src/kerf_cad_core/arch/bearing_wall_axial.py"; note: "ACI 318-19 §11.5.3.1 concrete/RC: φ·Pn=0.55·φ·f'c·Ag·[1−(k·lc/(32·t))²]; k: fixed_fixed=0.8, pin_pin=1.0, cantilever=2.0; valid for e≤t/6 (small-eccentricity bound); TMS 402-22 §8.3 Eq 8-22 masonry: r=t/√12; C_s=1−(h_eff/(140·r))² valid h_eff/r≤99; φ·Pn=φ·0.80·f'm·Ag·C_s; oracle t=200mm h=3000mm fc=25MPa pin-pin→slend=0.7803 φ·Pn=1394.7kN/m P=1000kN/m→DCR=0.717 (adequate); cantilever→φ·Pn=216.5kN/m; masonry f'm=10MPa pin-pin→r=57.7mm C_s=0.862 φ·Pn=896.7kN/m; e>t/6→large_eccentricity_method_required (PM interaction required); h/r>99→slenderness_limit_exceeded; arch_check_bearing_wall_axial LLM tool; SCOPE: ACI §11.5.3.1 EMPIRICAL only — large eccentricity requires full PM interaction (ACI §11.4); rebar NOT credited in empirical formula; TMS h/r≤99 limit; no in-plane shear (§11.6); Wight §13.13; 47 tests pass"

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels can classify rational quadratic NURBS as conics (degree-2 gate) but do not expose a general algebraic discriminant-based classifier for arbitrary-degree curves or raw point sets

kerf.note: detect_conic_type(curve_or_points, num_samples=100, residual_threshold_mm=0.01) -> ConicDetectResult(conic_type, conic_coefficients, eccentricity, rms_residual_mm, honest_caveat); fits general conic Ax²+Bxy+Cy²+Dx+Ey+F=0 via SVD null-space (Pratt 1987 + Fitzgibbon-Pilu-Fisher 1999); classifies by discriminant B²−4AC: circle|ellipse|parabola|hyperbola|line|free_form; eccentricity from 3×3 homogeneous matrix eigendecomposition; LLM tool nurbs_detect_conic_type. HONEST: algebraic fit is susceptible to noise bias — for production-quality circle detection use Pratt/Taubin (curve_circle_fit.py); parabola discrimination at discriminant boundary is fragile under noise.

features.name: Degree-5 NURBS curve fit with G2 curvature-continuous end conditions (P&T §9.4)

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels provide G1 (tangent) end-condition interpolation; G2 (curvature) endpoint matching for freeform curve fitting is less common and typically requires accessing lower-level knot/CP construction APIs

kerf.note: fit_curve_g2(spec: G2FitSpec, degree=5) -> G2FitResult; degree-5 B-spline fit to 3-D data points with G2 (curvature-continuous) end conditions; prescribe start_tangent_xyz, start_curvature_xyz, end_tangent_xyz, end_curvature_xyz; chord-length parameterisation + clamped averaging-rule knots + analytic endpoint CPs (P&T §9.4 Eqs 9.26-9.29); interior CPs by least-squares collocation; 22 hermetic tests (straight-line residual<1e-9, circle-arc residual<1e-6, G2 constraints<1e-9, G1 constraints<1e-9); LLM tool nurbs_fit_curve_g2. HONEST: chord-length only — centripetal parameterisation (Lee 1989) may give better results for high-aspect-ratio or irregularly-spaced data; curvature vectors are parametric C''(t), NOT Frenet kappa*n_hat — use adjacent curve's C''(t_join) for blending. Refs: Piegl & Tiller §9.4; Farin CAGD Ch 10.

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels can classify rational quadratic NURBS as conics (degree-2 gate) but do not expose a general algebraic discriminant-based classifier for arbitrary-degree curves or raw point sets

kerf.note: detect_conic_type(curve_or_points, num_samples=100, residual_threshold_mm=0.01) -> ConicDetectResult(conic_type, conic_coefficients, eccentricity, rms_residual_mm, honest_caveat); fits general conic Ax²+Bxy+Cy²+Dx+Ey+F=0 via SVD null-space (Pratt 1987 + Fitzgibbon-Pilu-Fisher 1999); classifies by discriminant B²−4AC: circle|ellipse|parabola|hyperbola|line|free_form; eccentricity from 3×3 homogeneous matrix eigendecomposition; LLM tool nurbs_detect_conic_type. HONEST: algebraic fit is susceptible to noise bias — for production-quality circle detection use Pratt/Taubin (curve_circle_fit.py); parabola discrimination at discriminant boundary is fragile under noise.

features.name: Degree-5 NURBS curve fit with G2 curvature-continuous end conditions (P&T §9.4)

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels provide G1 (tangent) end-condition interpolation; G2 (curvature) endpoint matching for freeform curve fitting is less common and typically requires accessing lower-level knot/CP construction APIs

kerf.note: fit_curve_g2(spec: G2FitSpec, degree=5) -> G2FitResult; degree-5 B-spline fit to 3-D data points with G2 (curvature-continuous) end conditions; prescribe start_tangent_xyz, start_curvature_xyz, end_tangent_xyz, end_curvature_xyz; chord-length parameterisation + clamped averaging-rule knots + analytic endpoint CPs (P&T §9.4 Eqs 9.26-9.29); interior CPs by least-squares collocation; 22 hermetic tests (straight-line residual<1e-9, circle-arc residual<1e-6, G2 constraints<1e-9, G1 constraints<1e-9); LLM tool nurbs_fit_curve_g2. HONEST: chord-length only — centripetal parameterisation (Lee 1989) may give better results for high-aspect-ratio or irregularly-spaced data; curvature vectors are parametric C''(t), NOT Frenet kappa*n_hat — use adjacent curve's C''(t_join) for blending. Refs: Piegl & Tiller §9.4; Farin CAGD Ch 10.

features.name: Degree-5 NURBS curve fit with G2 curvature-continuous end conditions (P&T §9.4)

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels provide G1 (tangent) end-condition interpolation; G2 (curvature) endpoint matching for freeform curve fitting is less common and typically requires accessing lower-level knot/CP construction APIs

kerf.note: fit_curve_g2(spec: G2FitSpec, degree=5) -> G2FitResult; degree-5 B-spline fit to 3-D data points with G2 (curvature-continuous) end conditions; prescribe start_tangent_xyz, start_curvature_xyz, end_tangent_xyz, end_curvature_xyz; chord-length parameterisation + clamped averaging-rule knots + analytic endpoint CPs (P&T §9.4 Eqs 9.26-9.29); interior CPs by least-squares collocation; 22 hermetic tests (straight-line residual<1e-9, circle-arc residual<1e-6, G2 constraints<1e-9, G1 constraints<1e-9); LLM tool nurbs_fit_curve_g2. HONEST: chord-length only — centripetal parameterisation (Lee 1989) may give better results for high-aspect-ratio or irregularly-spaced data; curvature vectors are parametric C''(t), NOT Frenet kappa*n_hat — use adjacent curve's C''(t_join) for blending. Refs: Piegl & Tiller §9.4; Farin CAGD Ch 10.

LLM tool: plm_assess_bom_maturity. Honest caveat: TRL weighted average is a simplified proxy — real assessments also require test heritage, supplier CMMI/AS9100 capability, FMEA closure, and PPAP evidence; a single low-TRL component can block a programme regardless of the mean score. References: NASA SP-2016-6105 Rev 2; ISO/IEC 15288:2023 §6.3; INCOSE SE Handbook v4 §4.1. 24 tests.

LLM tool: plm_export_change_log. Honest caveat: in-memory export only — no DB pagination for large change-log sets; all entries must fit in RAM. References: ISO 10007:2003 §6; PMI PMBOK 7th ed. §4.6. 24 tests.

features.name: Part obsolescence check (IEC 62402 DMSMS BOM risk)

competitor.notes: No built-in IEC 62402 obsolescence risk scoring; requires external DMSMS tool (SiliconExpert, IHS Markit, or custom spreadsheet)

kerf.notes: PartLifecycleStatus (active/preferred/NRND/LTB/EOL/obsolete); PartLifecycleEntry (part_number, manufacturer, status, last_buy_date, alternative_pn); check_part_obsolescence() — risk_score = (NRND×1 + LTB×3 + EOL×5 + obsolete×10) / total_parts × 10 (IEC 62402 §5.3 heuristic); critical_part_alerts for EOL and obsolete parts with alternative_pn suggestions; affected_assemblies from flat BOM relationships (deduplicated). LLM tool: plm_check_part_obsolescence. Honest caveat: lifecycle data is caller-supplied — no live IHS Markit / Octopart / Silicon Expert API; callers must refresh from distributor feeds / ERP / quarterly DMSMS scans; risk weights are kerf heuristics calibrated to DMSMS handbook §2.3; affected_assemblies is flat depth-1 only. References: IEC 62402:2019; US DoD DMSMS Handbook (2018) §2.3+§4.1; JEDEC JEP106. 26 tests.

kerf.notes: VariantRule (part_number, variant_attribute_key, variant_attribute_value, condition include|exclude); VariantSelection (variant_id, attributes dict); resolve_variant_bom() — first-match-per-part, open-world default (no matching rule → always included); VariantConfigReport (num_total_parts, num_included_parts, num_excluded_parts, resolved_bom). LLM tool: plm_resolve_variant_bom. Honest caveat: simple rule-based variant selection — NO formal feature-model solver, NO constraint propagation, NO mutual-exclusion groups; complex AND/OR/NOT variant expressions not supported in v1. References: PTC Windchill Variant Management; ISO 10303-44 §6; Saaksvuori & Immonen §4.3. 18 tests.

features.name: BOM revision compare diff (added / removed / qty changed)

competitor.notes: Windchill and Teamcenter include BOM redline reports; limited to licensed PLM suite — no standalone CLI/API; no structured JSON output

kerf.notes: BomLineItem (part_number, qty, description); compare_boms() diffs two flat BOMs keyed by part_number — classifies each line as added/removed/qty_increased/qty_decreased/unchanged; BomDiffReport (total_parts_old, total_parts_new, num_added, num_removed, num_qty_changed, num_unchanged, diff_entries sorted by part_number, honest_caveat); qty_delta signed; duplicate part_numbers collapsed (last-write-wins). LLM tool: plm_compare_boms. Honest caveat: flat single-level BOM diff only — no hierarchy traversal, no where-used cascade, no effectivity/variant expansion, no description-change detection, no PN alias normalisation. References: ISO 10303-44:2021 §6; PLM Dictionary 'BOM rev compare'. 18 tests.

competitor.notes: PDN Analyzer provides decoupling cap guidance; full SPICE-level simulation

kerf.notes: Z_target = ΔV/I (Howard Johnson §8.3); C_bulk = I·t_rise/ΔV (Ott §13.3); bypass 100 nF/10 nF per IC; max ESL per cap = Z_target/(2π·f_bw); honest: target-impedance heuristic only — PDN simulation (SPICE) required for anti-resonance validation

competitor.notes: Altium Interactive Length Tuning + DRC skew rules; KiCad PCB DRC diff-pair skew

kerf.notes: v = c/√εr (c=0.3 mm/ps); Δt = |ΔL|/v; budgets: HDMI 2.1=15 ps, USB 3.0=20 ps, PCIe 4.0=2 ps, DDR5=5 ps, SATA III=15 ps (Johnson §12.4 + IPC-2141A §6); electronics_check_diffpair_skew LLM tool; custom budget override. Honest: assumes uniform effective εr (±5–15% actual variation); meander-delay skew NOT modelled; via/connector transitions and inter-pair skew out of scope.

features.name: Pierce crystal oscillator load capacitor calculator (C1, C2)

competitor.notes: Altium oscillator wizard provides basic load-cap guidance; full simulation via SPICE

kerf.notes: CL=(C1·C2)/(C1+C2)+C_stray (NXP AN-2867 §3 + AVR ATmega §28.5); symmetric C1=C2=2·(CL−C_stray); asymmetric verification; qualitative gain-margin check (gm≥5×ESR per NXP AN-2867 §4.2); electronics_compute_crystal_load_caps LLM tool. Honest: drive-level limiting NOT modelled; PI-network for >20 MHz NOT applied; C_stray is a first-order estimate.

competitor.notes: HyperLynx EMC (separate licence); no built-in FCC/CISPR limit checker

kerf.notes: emc_wizard: FCC §15.109 + CISPR 32 (backend)

competitor.notes: Altium Designer has no built-in EMI power-line filter wizard; requires SPICE or external tools

kerf.notes: electronics_design_emi_filter: LC_low_pass (−40 dB/dec, f_c = f_t/10^(A/40), C=1/((2π·f_c)²·L)); PI_LC_L (π-section −60 dB/dec); RC_low_pass (−20 dB/dec); X2 E12 cap recommendation (IEC 60384-14). Honest: ideal passive only — no ESR/stray; DM-only; CISPR 22 Class B limits for context; accredited lab required for compliance. Refs: Ott §15.3; CISPR 22/EN 55022.

features.name: Buck DC-DC converter output voltage ripple (CCM)

competitor.notes: Altium Designer has no built-in CCM ripple calculator; requires SPICE/SIwave simulation

kerf.notes: electronics_compute_buck_ripple: D=V_out/V_in; ΔiL=(V_in−V_out)·D/(L·f_sw); ΔV_cap=ΔiL/(8·C·f_sw); ΔV_ESR=ΔiL·ESR; ΔV_out=ΔV_cap+ΔV_ESR (worst-case linear sum, Erickson §2.4). CCM validity flag (iL_min=I_load−ΔiL/2>0); DCM warning; ESR-dominance flag; small-ripple approximation warning >30% load variation. Honest: CCM only — DCM NOT modelled; D=V_out/V_in ideal (no deadtime/drop); ESR flat (use datasheet at f_sw); linear sum pessimistic vs RSS. Refs: Erickson 3e §2.4; Sandler §3.

competitor.notes: Altium Designer has no built-in MOSFET SOA checker; requires manual datasheet SOA graph lookup or external SPICE/LTspice simulation with SOA measurement macros

kerf.notes: electronics_check_fet_soa: P_diss=V_DS×I_D×duty; T_J=T_amb+P_diss×R_θJA (Jedec JESD51 single-node); violation checks: V_DSS_exceeded, I_D_continuous_exceeded (duty==1), I_D_pulsed_exceeded (duty<1), T_J_exceeded, P_D_exceeded; headroom_pct=(1−max_utilisation)×100. Honest: LINEARISED SOA only — real datasheet SOA curves are non-linear (IRF Hexfet Designer’s Manual §5 Fig 5-1); pulse-width SOA NOT interpolated (short pulses allow higher peak I_D×V_DS before thermal integral exceeded); second-breakdown/UIS NOT explicitly modelled (verify E_AS=½L·I_peak² separately); thermal model is single-node JESD51 steady-state R_θJA (not transient Z_θJC(t)); R_DS_on is room-temperature value (increases with T_J per Infineon AN 2015-11); switching losses NOT included. Refs: IRF Hexfet Designer’s Manual §5; IPC-9701A; Jedec JESD51-1; Infineon AN 2015-11.

features.name: LDO linear regulator dropout and thermal compliance check

competitor.notes: Altium Designer has no built-in LDO dropout checker; requires manual datasheet lookup or SPICE/LTspice simulation

kerf.notes: electronics_check_ldo_dropout: headroom_mV=(V_in_min−V_out)×1000; dropout_compliant=headroom>dropout_spec (strict); P_diss=(V_in_max−V_out)×I_load; T_j=T_ambient+P_diss×R_θja (Jedec JESD51 single-node); thermal_compliant=T_j<T_max_j; efficiency=100×V_out/V_in_max (pessimistic). Honest: quiescent current I_Q NOT included (dominates at light load); transient headroom NOT checked (add ≥100–200 mV guard-band for PSRR/load steps, Sandler §4.4); output capacitor stability NOT verified (ESR window); θja is worst-case single-node thermal model. Refs: TI Power Ref §3; Sandler §4; Jedec JESD51-1.

competitor.notes: Altium Designer has no built-in inductor core saturation checker; requires manual datasheet lookup or external magnetics simulation (e.g. Ansys Maxwell, FEMM)

kerf.notes: electronics_check_inductor_saturation: I_pk=I_dc+I_ripple_pp/2; B_pk=μ₀·μ_r·N·I_pk/l_e (Ampere's law, Erickson §15 + McLyman §10); saturation_margin=(B_sat−B_pk)/B_sat×100; saturated=B_pk>=B_sat; recommended_max_I_dc (1% guard-band). Ferrite B_sat derating: 100°C→−15%, 125°C→−25%, 150°C→−40%, 200°C→−50% (Ferroxcube 3C95/3F36). Materials: ferrite_3C95 (B_sat≈500mT, μ_r≈2000), ferrite_3F36 (≈380mT, ≈1500), powdered_iron_-26 (≈1400mT, ≈75), sendust (≈1000mT, ≈125), NiFe50 (≈1600mT, ≈3000). Honest: fringing flux ignored (gapped cores need Dowell/Erickson §15.3 correction); μ_r assumed constant (real ferrite μ_r rolls off in knee region BEFORE B_sat — model conservatively over-estimates B_pk; use nonlinear B-H curve for precision); non-ferrite B_sat derating NOT modelled; winding I²R NOT accounted; AC core losses (Steinmetz) NOT computed. Refs: Erickson 3e §15; McLyman 4e §10; Ferroxcube 3C95/3F36 datasheets; Magnetics Inc. Powder Core Catalog §3.

features.name: Op-amp offset voltage and temperature drift (precision instrumentation)

competitor.notes: Altium Designer has no built-in op-amp offset drift calculator; requires manual datasheet lookup or SPICE/LTspice Monte Carlo / temperature sweep simulation

kerf.notes: electronics_compute_op_amp_drift: Vos(T)=Vos_typ+TC_Vos×(T−Tref) (TI SLOA069 §3 Eq. 3-1); worst-case over all 4 ±drift×±endpoint combinations; Vos_max_IR=max(|Vos(T_min)|,|Vos(T_max)|); Vos_OR_mV=gain×Vos_max_IR/1000; error_pct=100×(Vos_OR/1000)/FS. Class thresholds: chopper≤0.05µV/°C (AD8551, MAX420), zero-drift≤0.1µV/°C (OPA188, LTC2050), precision≤1µV/°C (OPA227, LT1012), standard>1µV/°C. Honest: linear drift model only — real drift is non-linear and asymmetric (TI SLOA069 Fig 3-2); 1/f noise NOT modelled; PSRR/CMRR cross-talk OUT OF SCOPE; resistor TC mismatch NOT included; use Vos_max (not Vos_typ) for guaranteed worst-case. Refs: TI SLOA069 §3; Analog Devices AN-580 §1.

features.name: Zener clamp / shunt voltage regulator design

competitor.notes: Altium Designer has no built-in Zener shunt regulator calculator; requires manual SPICE simulation or external spreadsheet

kerf.notes: electronics_design_zener_clamp: R=(V_in_max−V_Z)/(I_load_min+I_knee); I_Z_max=(V_in_max−V_Z_max)/R−I_load_min; P_Z_design=V_Z×I_Z_max×1.25 (25% derating, Vishay AN-2014-3 §3); packages 0.4W/0.5W/1W/3W/5W; E12 series resistor (IEC 60063). Honest: SIMPLE LINEAR SHUNT ONLY — wasted power is constant regardless of load; Zener rZ NOT modelled (ΔV_out=rZ×ΔI_Z, typical 7–20 Ω for 5V/400mW); temperature coefficient of V_Z NOT modelled; for I_load>100 mA prefer LDO or buck converter; static regulation=2×tol% (dynamic excluded). Refs: H&H §2.2.4; Vishay AN-2014-3.

Full IPC-2152 corrected model also available (tracecurrent_ipc2152 — copper-weight correction cf_cw, board-k correction cf_th, plane-proximity correction cf_pl). competitor.notes: Altium has no native NEC 210.19(A) voltage-drop check for conductor runs; third-party scripts required

kerf.notes: NEC 2023 Article 210.19(A) voltage-drop check: DC/single-phase/three-phase; NEC Ch9 Table 8 resistance at 75°C (AWG 14–4/0 + 250 kcmil, Cu/Al); IEEE 141 §3 formulas; advisory ≤3% feeder / ≤2% branch check; electronics_check_voltage_drop LLM tool. Honest: 75°C baseline only; AC reactance ignored for short runs.

features.name: OCPD sizing check / NEC 240.4 + 215 circuit-protection compliance

competitor.notes: Altium has no NEC 240.4 OCPD sizing or conductor-ampacity compliance check; requires external NEC reference or spreadsheet

kerf.notes: NEC 2023 Article 240.4 + Table 310.16 + Article 215 circuit-protection check: NEC Table 310.16 75°C column (Cu + Al, AWG 14–500 kcmil, THWN/THHN/XHHW/RHW); required OCPD = 1.25 × I_continuous + I_non_continuous (NEC 215.3/210.20(A)); 240.4(B) OCPD ≤ ampacity; 240.4(D) small-conductor cap (14 AWG→15 A, 12→20 A, 10→30 A); electronics_check_circuit_protection LLM tool. Honest: 75°C THWN baseline; no ambient-temperature derating (NEC 310.15(B)(2)(a)); no bundling derating (NEC Table 310.15(B)(3)(a)); arc-flash and grounding out of scope.

features.name: Wire ampacity derating / NEC 310.15(B)(2)(a) ambient + 310.15(B)(3)(a) bundling

competitor.notes: Altium has no NEC 310.15 ambient-temperature or conductor-bundling derating calculator; requires external NEC reference or spreadsheet

kerf.notes: NEC 2023 Article 310 wire ampacity derating: ambient correction (Table 310.15(B)(2)(a), 75°C column: ≤30°C→1.00, 31–35→0.94, 36–40→0.88, 41–45→0.82, 46–50→0.75, 51–55→0.67, 56–60→0.58) + bundling adjustment (Table 310.15(B)(3)(a): 1–3→1.00, 4–6→0.80, 7–9→0.70, 10–20→0.50, 21–30→0.45, 31–40→0.40, 41+→0.35); effective = base × C_ambient × C_bundle; electronics_compute_derated_ampacity LLM tool. Honest: 75°C insulation column only (THWN/THHN/XHHW/RHW); rooftop adder, underground/buried, and Type NM cable derating NOT modelled; free-air (Table 310.17) not covered; ambient > 60°C out of range.

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels can classify rational quadratic NURBS as conics (degree-2 gate) but do not expose a general algebraic discriminant-based classifier for arbitrary-degree curves or raw point sets

kerf.note: detect_conic_type(curve_or_points, num_samples=100, residual_threshold_mm=0.01) -> ConicDetectResult(conic_type, conic_coefficients, eccentricity, rms_residual_mm, honest_caveat); fits general conic Ax²+Bxy+Cy²+Dx+Ey+F=0 via SVD null-space (Pratt 1987 + Fitzgibbon-Pilu-Fisher 1999); classifies by discriminant B²−4AC: circle|ellipse|parabola|hyperbola|line|free_form; eccentricity from 3×3 homogeneous matrix eigendecomposition; LLM tool nurbs_detect_conic_type. HONEST: algebraic fit is susceptible to noise bias — for production-quality circle detection use Pratt/Taubin (curve_circle_fit.py); parabola discrimination at discriminant boundary is fragile under noise.

features.name: Degree-5 NURBS curve fit with G2 curvature-continuous end conditions (P&T §9.4)

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels provide G1 (tangent) end-condition interpolation; G2 (curvature) endpoint matching for freeform curve fitting is less common and typically requires accessing lower-level knot/CP construction APIs

kerf.note: fit_curve_g2(spec: G2FitSpec, degree=5) -> G2FitResult; degree-5 B-spline fit to 3-D data points with G2 (curvature-continuous) end conditions; prescribe start_tangent_xyz, start_curvature_xyz, end_tangent_xyz, end_curvature_xyz; chord-length parameterisation + clamped averaging-rule knots + analytic endpoint CPs (P&T §9.4 Eqs 9.26-9.29); interior CPs by least-squares collocation; 22 hermetic tests (straight-line residual<1e-9, circle-arc residual<1e-6, G2 constraints<1e-9, G1 constraints<1e-9); LLM tool nurbs_fit_curve_g2. HONEST: chord-length only — centripetal parameterisation (Lee 1989) may give better results for high-aspect-ratio or irregularly-spaced data; curvature vectors are parametric C''(t), NOT Frenet kappa*n_hat — use adjacent curve's C''(t_join) for blending. Refs: Piegl & Tiller §9.4; Farin CAGD Ch 10.

features.name: Fuse I²t (melting energy) verification / IEC 60269 + Cooper Bussmann

competitor.notes: Altium Designer has no built-in fuse I²t verification or IEC 60269 pre-arcing energy check; requires external manufacturer datasheets and manual spreadsheet calculation

kerf.notes: electronics_check_fuse_i2t: applied I²t = I_peak² × t (square-wave); clears_safely = applied ≥ fuse pre-arcing I²t; breaking_capacity_adequate = available_SCC ≤ breaking_capacity_kA; fuse classes F/M/T/FF/gG/aR (IEC 60269-1). HONEST: square-wave fault current only — sinusoidal AC or exponentially decaying DC NOT modelled; arcing I²t NOT included; fuse co-ordination NOT checked. Refs: IEC 60269-1:2020; Cooper Bussmann SPD §2–§4.

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels can classify rational quadratic NURBS as conics (degree-2 gate) but do not expose a general algebraic discriminant-based classifier for arbitrary-degree curves or raw point sets

kerf.note: detect_conic_type(curve_or_points, num_samples=100, residual_threshold_mm=0.01) -> ConicDetectResult(conic_type, conic_coefficients, eccentricity, rms_residual_mm, honest_caveat); fits general conic Ax²+Bxy+Cy²+Dx+Ey+F=0 via SVD null-space (Pratt 1987 + Fitzgibbon-Pilu-Fisher 1999); classifies by discriminant B²−4AC: circle|ellipse|parabola|hyperbola|line|free_form; eccentricity from 3×3 homogeneous matrix eigendecomposition; LLM tool nurbs_detect_conic_type. HONEST: algebraic fit is susceptible to noise bias — for production-quality circle detection use Pratt/Taubin (curve_circle_fit.py); parabola discrimination at discriminant boundary is fragile under noise.

kerf.note: ST editor + live Ladder power-flow sim wired in-browser; chat-native ST generation

competitor.note: OpenPLC Runtime compiles IEC 61131-3 to C++, deploys to target; web dashboard for monitor

kerf.note: FirmwareActions + debug panel wired; build/upload/monitor/debug in-browser

competitor.note: OpenPLC has no wiring harness or cable design tooling

kerf.note: WiringView with WireViz + 3D harness router wired

competitor.note: OpenPLC has no power distribution or short-circuit analysis

kerf.note: NEC power distribution + point-to-point short-circuit (backend)

competitor.note: OpenPLC has no load-flow or power-systems analysis

kerf.note: Full polar-form Newton-Raphson load-flow; 3+5-bus validated (backend)

competitor.note: OpenPLC has no OTA delivery; firmware must be flashed manually per device

kerf.note: POST /firmware/ota/release (Ed25519-signed) + GET /firmware/ota/check; C backends for ESP32/STM32/SAMD

competitor.note: OpenPLC has no linker memory-map analysis; Flash/SRAM overflow is discovered only at link time

kerf.note: firmware_verify_memory_map: FLASH_OVERFLOW, SRAM_OVERFLOW, STACK_OVERFLOW_INTO_BSS, VECTOR_TABLE_MISPLACED (ARM Cortex-M UG §B3.2.4), ISR_COUNT_MISMATCH; STM32F411 512 KB Flash/128 KB SRAM (RM0383 §3) + STM32F407 1 MB/128 KB (RM0090 §3); 63 tests. STATIC SECTION-SIZE CHECK ONLY — does not run the linker.

competitor.note: OpenPLC has no solar PV or energy modelling capability

kerf.note: Single-diode + bypass-diode IV + global MPPT + mismatch loss + partial shading (backend)

competitor.note: OpenPLC has no protection coordination or arc-flash analysis

kerf.note: IEEE C37.112 U1-U5 TCC + IEEE 1584-2018 arc-flash incident energy (backend)

features.feature: MCU peripheral pin-map verifier (alt-function + conflict check)

competitor.note: OpenPLC has no MCU peripheral pin-mapping verifier; pin assignments must be checked manually in the IDE

kerf.note: firmware_verify_peripheral_map: checks pin alt-function, pin conflicts, missing required peripherals, peripheral mux conflicts, voltage compatibility (5V tolerant); embedded STM32F411 LQFP64 (RM0383 §7.3) + ATmega328P PDIP-28 (§13) tables; 59 tests. NOT a substitute for ST CubeMX / Atmel Start.

features.feature: MCU clock-tree verifier (PLL + AHB/APB + USB 48 MHz + ADC 36 MHz)

competitor.note: OpenPLC has no clock-tree configuration verifier

kerf.note: firmware_verify_clock_tree: real PLL arithmetic (VCO=HSE/PLLM×PLLN, SYSCLK=VCO/PLLP, USB=VCO/PLLQ); detects SYSCLK exceeded (100 MHz F411 / 168 MHz F407), APB1 >42 MHz, APB2 >84 MHz, VCO out of range, USB≠48 MHz (±500 ppm), ADC >36 MHz; embedded RM0383 §6 (STM32F411) + RM0090 §6 (STM32F407) tables; 71 tests. LOOKUP-TABLE ONLY — no jitter model.

features.feature: CAN bus load analyser (CAN 2.0B / J1939-21 utilisation check)

competitor.note: OpenPLC has no CAN bus load or utilisation analysis; bus sizing must be done manually

kerf.note: firmware_compute_can_bus_load: CAN 2.0B frame model (standard 11-bit: 47+8·DLC+24 avg stuffing bits; extended 29-bit: 67+8·DLC+24); per-message load breakdown; flags >30% conservative + >40% J1939-21 §5.2 threshold; 55 tests. AVERAGE stuffing estimate — add ≥10% headroom for worst-case.

features.feature: SPI master/slave timing compatibility verifier (CPOL/CPHA, clock rate, setup/hold margins)

competitor.note: OpenPLC has no SPI timing compatibility analysis; engineers must check datasheet timing manually

kerf.note: firmware_verify_spi_timing: Motorola SPI spec (canonical CPOL/CPHA) + ARM Cortex-M Generic UG §SPI Timing; checks clock_hz ≤ max_clk_hz, setup_ns ≥ min_setup_ns, hold_ns ≥ min_hold_ns, CPOL exact match, CPHA exact match; violation codes: CLOCK_TOO_FAST, SETUP_VIOLATION, HOLD_VIOLATION, CPOL_MISMATCH, CPHA_MISMATCH; MCP3008 ADC edge case (DS21295D §1.0 Table 1-1, 1.35 MHz max at 2.7V) tested against STM32F411 SPI1; 41 tests. HONEST: assumes ideal square-wave signals — ignores PCB trace propagation delay and crosstalk.

features.feature: I²C clock-stretch worst-case effective speed + SCL low-timeout compliance (NXP UM10204 §3.1.9)

competitor.note: OpenPLC has no I²C clock-stretch analysis; engineers must manually account for slave stretching and MCU timeout configuration

kerf.note: firmware_check_i2c_clock_stretch: NXP UM10204 Rev 7 §3.1.9 + ARM Cortex-M I²C timing; f_effective = 9/((9/f_nom)+stretch_us/1e6); timeout_compliant = (worst_stretch×bytes) < scl_low_timeout_ms×1000; reports effective_clock_hz, worst_case_stretch_per_byte_us, throughput_degradation_pct, slowest_slave; 36 tests. HONEST: single-master only — multi-master arbitration NOT modelled; stretch conservatively accumulated on every byte; rise/fall derating and t_BUF not included.

features.feature: UART baud-rate drift check (divisor/UBRR vs RS-232 ±2% tolerance)

competitor.note: No UART baud-rate drift analysis built into OpenPLC or any standard EDA suite

kerf.note: firmware_check_uart_baud_drift: ATmega-style integer-divisor model (ATmega328P §19 + STM32F411 RM0383 §19); baud=clock/(N×(UBRR+1)) where N=16 (normal) or 8 (double_speed); drift_pct=(actual−nominal)/nominal×100; reliable=|drift|<2% (RS-232/IEEE Std 488); recommendation engine scans standard bauds [9600..921600]×{normal,double_speed} for |drift|<0.5%; 24 tests. HONEST: integer-divisor model is exact for AVR (ATmega328P); for STM32 (fractional BRR) this model OVER-ESTIMATES drift — STM32 fractional generator achieves <0.1% on standard bauds whereas integer model reports ~0.94% at 80 MHz/115200; clock-source tolerance not modelled.

features.feature: Watchdog timeout interval check (IWDG/WDT: prescaler × (reload+1) / clock vs 2× worst-case loop latency)

competitor.note: No watchdog timeout interval verification built into OpenPLC or any standard EDA suite; engineers manually calculate IWDG/WDT timeout and check against loop latency

kerf.note: firmware_check_watchdog_interval: STM32F411 IWDG (RM0383 §17) + ATmega328P WDT (§11); timeout_s = prescaler×(reload+1)/clock_hz; adequate = timeout_ms > 2×worst_case_ms (ARM Keil AN259); reports actual_timeout_ms, headroom_ms, safety_margin_pct, adequate; recommends reload for 2.5× margin when inadequate; 40 tests. HONEST: assumes ideal LSI/internal RC at nominal frequency — STM32F411 LSI ±10–15% over temperature/process (RM0383 Table 54), ATmega WDT ±10–15%; real margin should subtract ≥10%. WWDG window-watchdog upper-bound NOT modelled.

features.feature: Flash wear-level endurance estimator (sector erase cycles, time-to-failure, recommended N-sector pool)

competitor.note: OpenPLC has no flash endurance or wear-level analysis; engineers manually calculate erase cycle budgets from datasheets

kerf.note: firmware_compute_flash_wear: total_writes = writes_per_second × 31,557,600 s/yr × lifetime_years; cycles_per_sector = ceil(total_writes / WA) / N; adequate = cycles_per_sector ≤ endurance_cycles; recommended_sectors = ceil(sector_erases / endurance); STM32F411 128 KB sector (RM0383 §3, 10k cycles), 1 write/s, 10yr, 1 sector → 3.15e8 cycles (inadequate, need ~31,558 sectors); ATmega328P EEPROM (§11, 100k cycles), 0.0001 write/s, 10yr → 31,558 cycles (adequate); 26 tests. HONEST: assumes perfect uniform wear distribution — real algorithms (NuttX MTD, Zephyr NVS, ST AN2594) achieve ~80–90% of ideal; WA model is worst-case; temperature derating not modelled.

features.feature: RAM utilisation audit (.data + .bss + heap + stack vs total SRAM, 80% budget, largest-contributor recommendation)

competitor.note: OpenPLC has no RAM utilisation analysis; engineers manually sum section sizes from arm-none-eabi-size output and compare against datasheet

kerf.note: firmware_audit_ram_usage: static_alloc=data+bss; dynamic_alloc=heap+stack; total_used=static+dynamic; within_budget=total_used≤80% of total_ram (10% safety guard); recommendation names largest contributor when over budget; depth-bar: STM32F411 128KB (RM0383 §2) data=8KB/bss=16KB/heap=24KB/stack=8KB → 56KB used, 43.75% → adequate; ATmega328P 2KB (§8) data=500B/bss=1200B/heap=200B/stack=100B → 2000B, 97.66% → over budget; 46 tests. HONEST: static estimate only — does not account for malloc fragmentation or interrupt-driven stack growth beyond declared stack_max_bytes.

features.feature: Static worst-case stack depth estimator (Cortex-M / ATmega, DFS call-tree, ISR overhead, cycle detection)

competitor.note: OpenPLC has no static stack depth analysis; engineers must manually trace call graphs from arm-none-eabi-gcc -fstack-usage output

kerf.note: firmware_estimate_stack_depth: DFS through call graph accumulating per-function frame sizes; detects recursive cycles (back-edges); ISR preemption overhead added once (default 32 B = Cortex-M3/M4/M7 exception frame: 8 regs × 4 B, ARM Generic UG §B1.5.6); depth-bar: main(100 B)→A(200 B)→B(50 B) + ISR 32 B = 382 B; cycle A→B→A flagged has_cycles=True; multiple-callee branching takes deepest path; ATmega variant isr_overhead_bytes=3 (PC save only); 38 tests. HONEST: static estimate only — does not account for VLA (variable-length arrays; exact depth is undecidable with VLAs), alloca(), longjmp/setjmp, or indirect calls; frame sizes must come from -fstack-usage *.su files, not guesses; recursive cycles report per-iteration lower bound only. Refs: ARM IHI 0042F AAPCS §5.2/§6.1; Greenwood (2011) ‘Static Stack Analysis for Embedded Systems’ §3.

features.feature: RTOS task priority check (Liu-Layland RM bound, hyperbolic bound, FreeRTOS/Zephyr/ChibiOS)

competitor.note: OpenPLC has no RTOS schedulability analysis; engineers must manually compute utilisation and verify rate-monotonic priority assignments

kerf.note: firmware_check_rtos_priorities: Liu-Layland (1973) RM bound U=Σ Cᵢ/Tᵢ ≤ n·(2^(1/n)−1); Bini-Buttazzo (2001) hyperbolic bound Π(Uᵢ+1)≤2; RM priority assignment check (shorter period → higher priority integer); depth-bar: 2 tasks T1 10ms/4ms P2, T2 20ms/8ms P1 → U=80%, bound=82.84%, schedulable, assignment correct; 43 tests. HONEST: sufficient condition for independent periodic tasks only — does NOT model shared resources (PCP/PIP), aperiodic tasks, interrupt latency, context-switch overhead, or Di≠Ti deadlines. Refs: Liu-Layland (1973) J. ACM 20(1); Bini-Buttazzo (2001) ECRTS; ARM FreeRTOS Kernel Docs; Renesas R01AN5545; Zephyr Scheduling Docs.

features.feature: CRC checksum computation (CRC-8, CRC-16/CCITT, CRC-16/MODBUS, CRC-32, CRC-32C)

competitor.note: OpenPLC has no CRC computation tooling; engineers must implement manually or use external tools

kerf.note: firmware_compute_crc: table-driven CRC-8 (poly 0x07), CRC-16/CCITT-FALSE (poly 0x1021, init 0xFFFF, ITU-T V.41), CRC-16/MODBUS (reflected 0xA001, init 0xFFFF, Modbus RTU §2.5.1), CRC-32/ISO-HDLC (reflected 0xEDB88320, IEEE 802.3), CRC-32C/Castagnoli (reflected 0x82F63B78, iSCSI RFC 3720 / SCTP RFC 4960); check-vector '123456789': CRC-16/CCITT=0x29B1, CRC-32=0xCBF43926, CRC-32C=0xE3069283; returns hex + int + zero-padded binary; 66 tests. HONEST: 5 standard algorithms only — CRC-64, CRC-16/CCITT-TRUE (init=0), CRC-32/BZIP2 (big-endian), and bitwise-reflection variants require a separate tool. Refs: Koopman (2002); ITU-T V.41; IEEE 802.3-2018 §3.2.8; RFC 3720 §B.4; Williams (1993).

features.feature: PWM resolution and frequency-error analysis (timer clock, target frequency, counter bit-width)

competitor.note: No PWM resolution or frequency-error analysis built into OpenPLC or any standard EDA suite; engineers manually compute ARR from datasheets

kerf.note: firmware_check_pwm_resolution: searches integer prescaler P ∈ [1..65536] to maximise resolution_bits=log2(ARR+1) with |freq_error|<1%; ARR=round(clock/(P×freq)−1) clamped to [1,2^bits−1]; oracle: 16 MHz/1 kHz/16-bit → P=1,ARR=15999,13.97 bits; 16 MHz/20 kHz/16-bit → P=1,ARR=799,9.64 bits; STM32F411 100 MHz/20 kHz/16-bit → P=1,ARR=4999,12.29 bits; 100 MHz/50 Hz/32-bit → >20 bits; 25 tests. HONEST: integer-prescaler model is exact for STM32F411 (PSC ∈ [0..65535], RM0383 §13) — ATmega328P Timer1 uses discrete {1,8,64,256,1024} only (§15); interrupt latency, dead-time insertion (TIM1/TIM8 BDTR), and complementary PWM NOT modelled. Refs: STM32F411 RM0383 §13 (TIM2–TIM5); ATmega328P §15 (Timer/Counter1 Fast PWM, ICR1 mode).

competitor.note: No ADC ENOB or oversampling analysis in OpenPLC or standard EDA suites; engineers manually apply ADI MT-003 formula from datasheets

kerf.note: firmware_compute_adc_enob: ENOB from SINAD via ADI MT-003 (ENOB=(SINAD−1.76)/6.02) or from datasheet enob_specified; oversampling gain ENOB_after=ENOB+log2(OSR)/2 (TI SBAA221 §2.1 — each 4× OSR adds 1 bit); voltage resolution=signal_full_scale_V/2^ENOB_after; recommended OSR=4^(target−ENOB) rounded up to next power-of-4; SNR=6.02×ENOB_after+1.76; depth-bars: SINAD=68 dB → ENOB≈11.00 bits; OSR=16 → +2.0 bits → 13.00 bits; target 14 from ENOB 11 → recommend OSR=64; 58 tests. HONEST: oversampling gain assumes white noise ONLY — correlated sources (50/60 Hz mains hum, PSU ripple, reference noise, quantisation plateaux on DC signals) do NOT average out; real-world gain is typically 0.5–1.5 bits per OSR decade, not theoretical log2(OSR)/2; kT/C thermal noise sets an absolute floor; dither injection partially recovers gain. Refs: ADI MT-003 Rev. B; TI SBAA221; Maxim AN2861.

NOT a substitute for ST CubeMX / Atmel Start.

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels can classify rational quadratic NURBS as conics (degree-2 gate) but do not expose a general algebraic discriminant-based classifier for arbitrary-degree curves or raw point sets

kerf.note: detect_conic_type(curve_or_points, num_samples=100, residual_threshold_mm=0.01) -> ConicDetectResult(conic_type, conic_coefficients, eccentricity, rms_residual_mm, honest_caveat); fits general conic Ax²+Bxy+Cy²+Dx+Ey+F=0 via SVD null-space (Pratt 1987 + Fitzgibbon-Pilu-Fisher 1999); classifies by discriminant B²−4AC: circle|ellipse|parabola|hyperbola|line|free_form; eccentricity from 3×3 homogeneous matrix eigendecomposition; LLM tool nurbs_detect_conic_type. HONEST: algebraic fit is susceptible to noise bias — for production-quality circle detection use Pratt/Taubin (curve_circle_fit.py); parabola discrimination at discriminant boundary is fragile under noise.

features.name: Degree-5 NURBS curve fit with G2 curvature-continuous end conditions (P&T §9.4)

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels provide G1 (tangent) end-condition interpolation; G2 (curvature) endpoint matching for freeform curve fitting is less common and typically requires accessing lower-level knot/CP construction APIs

kerf.note: fit_curve_g2(spec: G2FitSpec, degree=5) -> G2FitResult; degree-5 B-spline fit to 3-D data points with G2 (curvature-continuous) end conditions; prescribe start_tangent_xyz, start_curvature_xyz, end_tangent_xyz, end_curvature_xyz; chord-length parameterisation + clamped averaging-rule knots + analytic endpoint CPs (P&T §9.4 Eqs 9.26-9.29); interior CPs by least-squares collocation; 22 hermetic tests (straight-line residual<1e-9, circle-arc residual<1e-6, G2 constraints<1e-9, G1 constraints<1e-9); LLM tool nurbs_fit_curve_g2. HONEST: chord-length only — centripetal parameterisation (Lee 1989) may give better results for high-aspect-ratio or irregularly-spaced data; curvature vectors are parametric C''(t), NOT Frenet kappa*n_hat — use adjacent curve's C''(t_join) for blending. Refs: Piegl & Tiller §9.4; Farin CAGD Ch 10.

features.name: NURBS surface shear-transform offset (linear warp compensation)

competitor.note: No native global linear shear-transform on NURBS surface control points for machining warp compensation

kerf.note: apply_shear_offset(surface, ShearMatrix) -> SurfaceShearOffsetResult; six off-diagonal shear coefficients (s_xy, s_xz, s_yx, s_yz, s_zx, s_zy); P'=(x+s_xy*y+s_xz*z, y+s_yx*x+s_yz*z, z+s_zx*x+s_zy*y); knots and weights preserved exactly (P&T §6.1 Theorem 6.1); max/mean displacement metrics; LLM tool nurbs_apply_surface_shear_offset; 20 hermetic tests. HONEST: global linear shear only — non-uniform warp (thermal distortion, springback, gravity sag) requires per-point deformation, not a ShearMatrix. Refs: Mortenson §4.8; Piegl & Tiller §6.1.

features.name: NURBS curve footprint on plane (orthographic projection to 2-D)

competitor.note: No native NURBS-curve-to-plane perpendicular projection producing a 2-D footprint NurbsCurve in UV coordinates

kerf.note: project_curve_to_plane(curve, plane_point, plane_normal) -> FootprintResult; orthographic (linear) projection preserving degree, knots, and weights exactly (P&T §6.1 Theorem 6.1); FootprintResult.footprint_curve_2d is a NurbsCurve in plane-local UV coordinates; degenerate flag for perpendicular curves; lift_footprint_to_3d inverse; LLM tool nurbs_curve_project_to_plane; 17 hermetic tests. HONEST: orthographic only — no perspective; for rational NURBS the footprint CPs are correct homogeneous images but the Euclidean evaluated curve may differ from a naive point-by-point projection. Refs: Piegl & Tiller §6.1; Mortenson §4.4.

competitor.note: No built-in clothoid / Euler-spiral transition curve or arc-length curvature-rate re-parameterization

kerf.note: fresnel_parameterize_curve(): sampling-based (not closed-form) re-parameterization imposing κ(s)≈α·s Euler-spiral law; Fresnel integral C(σ)/S(σ) via scipy.special or Simpson fallback; monotone Fresnel-C parameter sequence drives degree-3 NURBS re-interpolation; max_curvature_residual metric; LLM tool nurbs_fresnel_parameterize_curve; 25 hermetic tests. HONEST: approximate (not closed-form Walton-Meek G1 clothoid spline); residual grows for long curves with large curvature rates. Refs: Walton & Meek (2009); Bertolazzi & Frego (2015).

features.name: NURBS curve evolute (locus of osculating-circle centres)

competitor.note: No built-in evolute computation for NURBS curves

kerf.note: compute_curve_evolute(curve, num_samples=200, min_curvature=1e-6) -> EvoluteResult; E(t)=C(t)+n̂(t)/κ(t) via analytical NURBS 1st/2nd derivatives; windowed local-extremum cusp detection with amplitude guard; circle oracle ≤ 1.6e-15 of center; parabola apex (0,1) exact; ellipse a=2,b=1 gives 4 cusps (stretched astroid); straight line gives empty evolute; LLM tool nurbs_compute_curve_evolute registered (gated import); 20 hermetic tests. HONEST: 2D curves only — 3D Frenet-Serret evolutes (torsion component) not yet supported; cusp detection windowed/amplitude-gated, depends on sample density. Applications: cycloidal-gear design, cam-profile cusps, CNC offset analysis. Refs: do Carmo §1.6; Mortenson §4.2.

features.name: NURBS curve inflection points (κ sign-change detection)

competitor.note: No built-in inflection-point detection for NURBS curves

kerf.note: find_curve_inflections(curve, num_samples=200, threshold=1e-9) -> InflectionResult; κ_signed(t)=(x'y''-y'x'')/|C'|³ sampled at num_samples uniform t-values; sign-change detection with threshold guard; 5-iteration bisection per bracket; signed_curvature_samples profile; LLM tool nurbs_find_curve_inflections registered (gated import); 20 hermetic tests. HONEST: 2D only — near-zero-curvature regions may produce false positives; gentle crossings may be missed at low threshold. Applications: fairness analysis, sketch QC, toolpath transitions. Refs: do Carmo §1.5; Sapidis §3.

features.name: B-rep vertex edge-degree histogram + non-manifold/boundary flagging

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels expose vertex valence internally but do not surface it as a standalone diagnostic API. Parasolid/OCCT provide low-level traversal; no first-class vertex-degree histogram or repair-triage tool.

kerf.note: check_vertex_degrees(brep_or_mesh, expected_degree=4) counts incident edges per vertex; flags boundary (degree < expected) and non-manifold (degree > expected+2); returns VertexDegreeReport with degree_histogram, num_boundary_vertices, num_non_manifold_vertices, max_degree, irregular_vertex_indices (capped 500), honest_caveat. Accepts face-list dicts or Body objects. LLM tool brep_check_vertex_degrees. Oracle-verified: cube {3:8}/no irregular at expected=3; tetrahedron {3:4}; 6-fan non-manifold; open mesh 2 boundary. HONEST: edge-based degree only — does not analyse face-fan angular order or non-manifold edges at high-degree vertices. Refs: Mantyla 1988 S3.4; Hoffmann 1989 S4.

features.feature: G83 peck-drill live-time analytics (chip-evacuation + tool-life budgeting)

competitor.note: Fusion 360 CAM / HSMWorks do not expose per-cycle cutting live-time fraction or chip-evacuation adequacy flag; retract overhead is implicit in time estimates only

kerf.note: compute_peck_live_time: PeckCycleParams + PeckLiveTimeReport; num_pecks = ceil(depth/peck); per-peck rapid-down + feed + rapid-up + dwell; live_time_fraction = cutting_s/total_s; adequate flag (threshold 0.50 per Sandvik CoroPlus 2024); cam_compute_peck_live_time LLM tool; 52 tests. HONEST: ideal rigid-rapid — no acceleration ramps at peck reversal (real machines +5–20 % retract overhead); full-retract G83 model only (G73 partial-retract not modelled). Refs: MH 31e §1132; Sandvik CoroPlus Drill Cycle Analytics 2024; NIST RS-274/NGC §3.8.4

competitor.note: G84 right-hand and G74 left-hand tapping cycles; rigid and floating-holder modes; post-processor configurable

kerf.note: NIST RS-274/NGC §3.8.4 G84 (right-hand) + G74 (left-hand); feed = pitch × rpm per MH 31e §1934; M29 Fanuc rigid-tap engage; cam_generate_tap_cycle LLM tool. Honest caveat: rigid tap timing only — floating tap holders not modelled; acceleration ramps excluded from time estimate

features.feature: Rigid-tap pre-run check (sync + torque + breakage risk)

competitor.note: CAM tools validate feed/speed but do not expose per-operation spindle sync compliance or tap breakage risk as a structured check; requires manual review of machine limits

kerf.note: check_rigid_tap(RigidTapSpec) → RigidTapReport: T = K·D³ torque (Sandvik CoroPlus 2024 + MH 31e §1934 empirical K; HSS-steel=0.35, cobalt-steel=0.30, carbide-steel=0.25, HSS-aluminum=0.10; 4 materials × 3 tool classes); F = pitch × rpm; sync_compliant = rpm ≤ machine_max_sync_rpm; tap_breakage_risk low/medium/high; recommended_feed_override_pct; metric + UNC/UNF thread parser; cam_check_rigid_tap LLM tool; 58 tests. Honest caveat: K coefficients ±35 % — coolant, tap geometry (spiral-flute vs spiral-point), thread-fit class, and worn tool state NOT modelled

competitor.note: G85/G86/G89 boring cycles; configurable dwell, feed-out, spindle-stop modes; post-processor configurable

kerf.note: NIST RS-274/NGC §3.8.4 G85 (feed-out, Ra 0.8 μm) + G86 (spindle-stop-out, Ra 1.6 μm) + G89 (dwell-out, Ra 0.4 μm) per MH 31e §1162; cam_generate_boring_cycle LLM tool; mixed cycle types per hole; G89 P-word in microseconds (NIST) — Fanuc uses milliseconds (verify before run). Honest caveats: single-point boring bar only — multi-edge heads out of scope; no roughing/finishing pass differentiation; acceleration ramps excluded from time estimate

competitor.note: Grinding wheel dress cycles available in Fusion 360 Manufacture for grinding operations; dresser traverse rate, depth, and pass count configurable; closed-loop dress compensation via sensor integration in premium setups

kerf.note: generate_dress_cycle(DressSpec) → DressCycleResult: num_passes = ceil(total_dress_amount / depth_per_pass); per pass: G01 infeed X + G01 traverse Z at traverse_rate + G00 rapid-retract Z; G28 reference return after last pass; dresser_type = 'single_point_diamond' (50–300 mm/min traverse, 0.002–0.025 mm/pass per MH 31e §1145) or 'rotary_diamond' (100–800 mm/min, 0.005–0.050 mm/pass per Sandvik CoroGrind §5); out-of-band advisory if spec outside safe bands; cam_generate_dress_cycle LLM tool; 58 tests green. HONEST: open-loop only — no AE sensor / power monitoring / roundness-gauging closed-loop correction; uni-directional traverse (+Z) only; no rotary-roll spindle sync G-code; acceleration ramps excluded from time estimate (add 10–20%); Fanuc 0i-PD / RS-274/NGC G-code — Siemens CYCLE12x / ANCA DressPro NOT emitted

features.feature: G04 dwell audit for milling programs

competitor.note: Fusion 360 CAM does not provide a dedicated G04 dwell audit or dwell-to-cutting-time ratio analysis; dwell commands can be inserted manually but no automatic flag for excessive or suspicious dwells

kerf.note: audit_milling_dwells(DwellAuditSpec) → DwellAuditReport: parses G04 X<seconds> (NIST RS-274/NGC §3.5) + G04 P<ms> (Fanuc/Haas); estimates G01 cutting time from distance/feed modal state; total_dwell_time_ms, num_dwells, dwell_per_op_ms, total_program_time_estimate_s, dwell_ratio_pct, excessive flag (ratio > max_total_dwell_ratio_pct, default 5 %), suspicious_long_dwells (> max_recommended_dwell_per_op_ms, default 500 ms); modal G20/G21, G90/G91, G94/G95 tracked; cam_audit_milling_dwells LLM tool; 45 tests. HONEST: heuristic only — some dwells are deliberate (G89 boring settle, machine warm-up, coolant pressure build); excessive=True is not proof of error; G02/G03 arc moves excluded from cutting-time estimate; rapid time assumed 10 000 mm/min generic constant; G95 feed-per-rev blocks contribute 0. Refs: MH 31e §1140; NIST RS-274/NGC §3.5

features.feature: Coolant flow rate and pressure adequacy check

competitor.note: Fusion 360 CAM links to CoroPlus ToolGuide for coolant recommendations but does not provide an in-app flow-rate vs MRR adequacy check or minimum pressure verification for through-tool systems

kerf.note: check_coolant_flow(MachiningOpForCoolant) → CoolantFlowReport: required_flow = flow_factor × MRR; factors (Sandvik CoroPlus §3): steel=0.50, stainless=0.60, aluminum=0.30, titanium=0.50, composite=0.20 L/min per cm³/min; pressure thresholds: titanium ≥70 bar (mandate), through-tool d>8mm ≥20 bar, d≤8mm ≥10 bar, flood ≥2 bar, mist ≥4 bar, MQL ≥5 bar; material-specific recommended_coolant_type (titanium→through_tool, stainless→through_tool, steel→flood, aluminum→flood, composite→MQL); flow_adequate + pressure_adequate flags; cam_check_coolant_flow LLM tool; 41 tests green. HONEST: heuristic flow factors only — coolant chemistry, concentration, and chip-evacuation effectiveness out of scope; seal integrity of through-tool spindle channels not verified; MQL/mist flow is lubricant volumetric flow, not flood; composite may require dry/MQL regardless of flow rate. Refs: Sandvik CoroPlus Coolant Guide §3 (2024); MH 31e §1140

features.feature: G02/G03 arc linearisation for legacy controllers

competitor.note: Fusion 360 CAM post-processors can output linear-only toolpaths for specific controllers via post-processor configuration, but there is no general-purpose in-app G02/G03-to-G01 conversion tool with configurable chord-error tolerance

kerf.note: linearize_arcs(ArcLinearizeSpec) → ArcLinearizeResult: converts G02/G03 arc commands to G01 linear segments for legacy CNC controllers (older Mach3 setups, plasma tables, laser cutters, wire EDM); I/J centre-offset + R radius formats both supported (R>0 minor arc, R<0 major arc per RS-274/NGC §3.5.3); full-circle I/J arcs handled; helical arcs (Z travel on G02/G03) linearised with linear Z interpolation; Δθ = 2·acos(1 − ε/R) per MH 31e §1130; min_segment_length_mm floor prevents micro-moves; returns linearized_gcode, num_arcs_processed, total_segments_emitted, max_actual_chord_error_mm, expansion_ratio; cam_linearize_arcs LLM tool; 42 tests green. HONEST: modal G-codes not preserved (explicit G01 each segment — downstream code must not assume modal state after linearised arc block); XY-plane (G17) arcs only — G18/G19 arcs pass through unchanged; full-circle R-format arcs ambiguous — passed through with warning comment; feed rate F copied verbatim to each segment. Refs: NIST RS-274/NGC §3.5.3; MH 31e §1130

competitor.note: Parallel / zig-zag facing strategies with stepover control; climb/conventional toggle; helical ramp entry available

kerf.note: MH 31e §1136 zig-zag face mill; 70 % stepover default; climb vs conventional; NIST RS-274/NGC §3.5 G-code output; cam_generate_face_mill_path LLM tool; 71 tests. Honest limits: 2.5D only (single flat depth); no helical entry; no G41/G42 cutter comp; no high-feed (HFC) strategies; no corner arcs at reversal; time estimate excludes acceleration ramps

features.feature: Lead-in / lead-out arc segments (witness mark prevention)

competitor.note: Tangent-arc, perpendicular, and smooth lead-in/out strategies for all 2D contour ops; configurable radius and angle; 3D ramp and helical entry also supported

kerf.note: MH 31e §1131 tangent-arc lead-in/lead-out; 3 types: arc (G02/G03 with I/J, sweep α=1–90°), line (G01 along tangent), perpendicular (G01 normal offset); G41/G42 cutter compensation activated in lead-in block (G40 cancel on exit); lead length = R·α_rad; cam_generate_lead_in_out LLM tool; 54 tests. Honest limits: 2D contours only (XY plane, constant Z) — 3D ramp entry and helical entry NOT implemented; no gouge/collision check; I/J incremental (Fanuc convention)

competitor.note: Feed-per-tooth and chip-thinning shown in CAM workspace

kerf.note: compute_chip_load(MillingOpSpec) → ChipLoadReport: fz = Vf/(n×z) (MH 31e §1136); Krad = D/(2·sqrt(D·ae−ae²)) when ae<D/2 else 1.0 (Sandvik CoroPlus 2024; Stephenson-Agapiou §6); actual_chip_load = fz×Krad; MRR = ae×ap×Vf/1000 cm³/min; Sandvik recommended ranges (carbide/aluminium 0.05–0.15, carbide/steel 0.05–0.20, HSS/aluminium 0.03–0.10, HSS/steel 0.03–0.12, ceramic 0.10–0.30 mm/tooth); compliant flag; cam_compute_chip_load LLM tool; 32 tests green. Honest caveats: thinning formula assumes circular arc-of-engagement (flat workpiece, peripheral end-mill only) — ball-nose and curved workpieces change engagement geometry; no climb/conventional distinction; tool deflection NOT modelled

competitor.note: Fusion 360 CAM turning workspace computes recommended DOC and pass strategy per material and insert grade via CoroPlus ToolGuide integration

kerf.note: compute_turning_depth(TurningSpec) → TurningDepthReport: radial_stock = (D_stock−D_final)/2; num_roughing = ceil((radial_stock−finish_doc)/effective_max_doc); roughing_doc = stock_for_roughing/num_roughing (equal passes); max_doc clamped to Sandvik CoroPlus / MH 31e §1148 material max (steel_1018 2–4 mm rec 3.0, aluminum_6061 4–8 mm rec 6.0, stainless_303 1.5–2.5 mm rec 2.0); nose-radius check finish_doc ≥ 0.5×r_ε; time_per_pass = L/(f×n/60); cam_compute_turning_depth LLM tool; 38 tests green. HONEST: straight (cylindrical) turning only — no profile turning, no taper, no threading, no facing; CSS/G96 NOT implemented; time estimate excludes rapids, tool changes, acceleration ramps

competitor.note: Parallel / scallop / radial 3D surface strategies with constant stepover and configurable scallop height; full gouge detection and 5-axis tilt available

kerf.note: MH 31e §1139 + Held & Klingenstein (1991) 3-axis Z-axis-aligned parallel-offset raster; Offset3DSpec + Offset3DResult + generate_offset_3d_path(spec); scallop h = R−sqrt(R²−(ae/2)²) (Chuang & Yang 1995); bilinear z(x,y) interpolation; cam_generate_offset_3d_path LLM tool; 71 tests. HONEST LIMITS: 3-axis only (no 5-axis tilt; steep walls >45° may micro-gouge); no gouge checking; raster passes only (no waterline/z-level contouring); G01 linear segments only (no arc fitting); scallop formula is flat-surface first-order estimate

features.feature: GD&T Feature of Size DOF enumerator — constrained vs released DOF per tolerance (ASME Y14.5-2018 §4.7 + §7.3)

competitor.note: Fusion 360 does not enumerate constrained/released degrees of freedom per FOS + tolerance type programmatically for inspection planning

kerf.note: compute_fos_dof(FOSSpec) -> FOSDoFReport: cylinder/hole+position→TX,TY; cylinder/hole+perpendicularity/parallelism/angularity→RX,RY; cylinder/hole+runout/total_runout→TX,TY,RX,RY (§7.3.4/§7.3.5 coupling); slot/planar_pair+position→TX; slot+orientation→RX,RY; sphere+position→TX,TY,TZ; sphere+orientation→nothing. gdt_compute_fos_dof LLM tool; 35 tests. HONEST FLAG: feature-class lookup table only — does not handle pattern-of-features compositions (§11.3) or PLTZF/FRTZF interactions (§10.5).

features.feature: GD&T datum shift check — MMC/LMC modifier bonus tolerance (ASME Y14.5-2018 §4.5 + §7.3.5)

competitor.note: Fusion 360 does not compute per-datum shift (bonus tolerance) for MMC/LMC-modified datum features of size programmatically

kerf.note: compute_datum_shift(DatumFeatureSpec, base_tol_mm): MMC shift=|measured-MMC|; LMC shift=|measured-LMC|; RFS shift=0; total=base+shift. DatumShiftReport carries bonus_shift_mm, total_available_tolerance_mm, shift_allowed. gdt_compute_datum_shift LLM tool registered. Per-feature check; multi-datum DRF secondary/tertiary cascade computed datum-by-datum (§4.11.4); composite frame validation (§10.5.2 PLTZF/FRTZF) is separate. 30 tests.

features.feature: GD&T composite tolerance frame (ASME Y14.5-2018 §10.5.2 PLTZF/FRTZF + §11.6)

competitor.note: Fusion 360 does not validate §10.5 composite frame PLTZF/FRTZF rules programmatically

kerf.note: validate_composite_frame(CompositeFrameSpec): R1 all segments same symbol (§10.5.2(a)); R2 FRTZF tol ≤ PLTZF tol (§10.5.1 Note 2); R3 FRTZF datum_refs ⊆ PLTZF datum_refs — no location datums below PLTZF (§10.5.1(b)); multi-tier 3+ segments; gdt_validate_composite_frame LLM tool; 44 tests. HONEST FLAG: validates frame structure only — does not verify inspection data against tolerance zones.

features.feature: GD&T circular and total runout compliance check (ASME Y14.5-2018 §13 / ISO 1101:2017 §18)

competitor.note: Fusion 360 does not provide a programmatic runout compliance checker that takes inspection point measurements and returns max_runout, FoM, and per-section breakdown

kerf.note: check_runout(RunoutCheckSpec, list[InspectionPoint]) -> RunoutCheckReport: circular runout (§13.2/ISO §18.3) = max FIM per z-section (max(R)−min(R)); total runout (§13.3/ISO §18.4) = FIM over all points simultaneously; fom = max_runout/tolerance; compliant when fom ≤ 1.0. Eccentric-cylinder oracle: 2d offset=0.10 mm confirmed. Tapered-cylinder oracle: circular_runout=0, total_runout=0.20 mm. gdt_check_runout LLM tool registered. 26 tests. HONEST FLAG: ideal datum axis assumed — Chebyshev-optimal (minimum zone) axis fit (ASME B89.3.1 / ISO 12181-1 §4.3) not computed; axial deviation for face runout surfaces not checked.

features.feature: GD&T datum reference frame validator (ASME Y14.5-2018 §4.11)

features.feature: GD&T datum precedence consistency analyser (ASME Y14.5-2018 §4.7 + §4.11)

competitor.note: Fusion 360 does not analyse cross-frame datum precedence consistency programmatically

kerf.note: analyze_datum_precedence_consistency: P1 feature-type conflict (ERROR); P2 precedence reversal A|B|C vs B|A|C (WARNING, §4.11); P3 DOF over-constraint >6 (ERROR, §4.7); P4 modifier conflict RMB/MMB/LMB (WARNING, §4.11.5). PrecedenceWarning carries code, severity, rule, affected_frames, recommendation. gdt_warn_datum_precedence LLM tool. HONEST FLAG: declared datum properties only. 41 tests.

features.feature: Where-Used Analysis — inverse BOM traversal (PROSTEP-iViP SIG §5.2)

competitor.note: Where-used available only through full PDM/PLM add-ons (e.g. SOLIDWORKS PDM, Fusion Manage, 3DExperience); not available in base modeller.

kerf.note: Where-Used Analysis: reverse-traverses the assembly hierarchy from a target part. Returns all parent assemblies with occurrence multiplicity and depth (depth=1 immediate parent, depth=2 grandparent, …). Cycle detection with cap at depth=20. LLM tool: plm_where_used.

features.feature: Component Where-Used Report — flat BOM inverse traversal with qty and level (ISO 10303-44 + APICS 'where-used')

competitor.note: Component where-used available only through full PDM/PLM add-ons; not natively available in base modeller from flat BOM input.

kerf.note: Given a flat list of BomRelationship(parent_pn, child_pn, qty), BFS upward from queried component to root assemblies. WhereUsedReport: component_pn, num_unique_parents, num_total_usages, max_depth, entries (WhereUsedEntry: parent_pn, parent_name, qty, level). Qty aggregation: multiple rows for same (parent, child) summed. Diamond BOM: shallowest BFS level wins. Cycle detection raises ValueError. Honest-flagged: in-memory traversal — no DB pagination, live PDM sync, effectivity filtering, or path-multiplied extended-qty. LLM tool: plm_component_whereused. 17 tests.

features.feature: Document Version Diff — controlled document delta (ISO 10303-44 §5.2 + Borst-Lahti §6.3)

competitor.note: No built-in document revision diff with field-level criticality classification; requires external PLM/PDM or custom scripting.

kerf.note: Field-level diff for BOM, drawing metadata, or any JSON-like doc revision: added, removed, modified (field-level criticality: engineering vs administrative), renamed (>=80% Jaccard heuristic). 100-item diff < 1 ms. LLM tool: plm_document_version_diff.

features.feature: Multi-Cavity Tool Effectivity — per-cavity insert revision tracking (PROSTEP-iViP SIG §6)

competitor.note: No built-in per-cavity insert effectivity tracking; multi-cavity tooling managed through external tool databases (DataM, ToolMonitor) or custom ERP; not natively modelled in base CAD/PLM.

kerf.note: Per-cavity insert revision state for multi-cavity injection molds: each cavity slot carries an insert history with date-effectivity windows. Query (tool, date) → [(cavity_id, revision), …]. Cavity-3 mid-run swap: query 2026-05-01 → [(1,R5),(2,R5),(3,R6),(4,R5)]; query 2026-03-15 → all R5. Honest flag: no insert wear / change-out queuing modelled. LLM tool: plm_query_multi_cavity.

features.feature: ECN Cascading Impact Analysis — BFS upward BOM traversal, drawings, work orders, heuristic cost (ISO 10007 §6 + APICS 'ECN')

competitor.note: Most CAD/PLM tools do not offer automated ECN cascading impact analysis from a flat BOM input; typically requires full PLM suite (Windchill, Teamcenter, Enovia).

kerf.note: analyze_ecn_impact: BFS upward from all ECN-affected components; deduped parent set; drawings + work-order counts; heuristic cost estimate (parents×$50 + drawings×$150 + WOs×$200); ISO 10007 §6.3.2 class assignment (Class_I_immediate / Class_II_rev / Class_III_drawing_only). Honest-flagged: cost is heuristic — real ECN costs vary widely; use for prioritisation only. LLM tool: plm_analyze_ecn_impact. 23 tests.

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels can classify rational quadratic NURBS as conics (degree-2 gate) but do not expose a general algebraic discriminant-based classifier for arbitrary-degree curves or raw point sets

kerf.note: detect_conic_type(curve_or_points, num_samples=100, residual_threshold_mm=0.01) -> ConicDetectResult(conic_type, conic_coefficients, eccentricity, rms_residual_mm, honest_caveat); fits general conic Ax²+Bxy+Cy²+Dx+Ey+F=0 via SVD null-space (Pratt 1987 + Fitzgibbon-Pilu-Fisher 1999); classifies by discriminant B²−4AC: circle|ellipse|parabola|hyperbola|line|free_form; eccentricity from 3×3 homogeneous matrix eigendecomposition; LLM tool nurbs_detect_conic_type. HONEST: algebraic fit is susceptible to noise bias — for production-quality circle detection use Pratt/Taubin (curve_circle_fit.py); parabola discrimination at discriminant boundary is fragile under noise.

features.name: Degree-5 NURBS curve fit with G2 curvature-continuous end conditions (P&T §9.4)

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels provide G1 (tangent) end-condition interpolation; G2 (curvature) endpoint matching for freeform curve fitting is less common and typically requires accessing lower-level knot/CP construction APIs

kerf.note: fit_curve_g2(spec: G2FitSpec, degree=5) -> G2FitResult; degree-5 B-spline fit to 3-D data points with G2 (curvature-continuous) end conditions; prescribe start_tangent_xyz, start_curvature_xyz, end_tangent_xyz, end_curvature_xyz; chord-length parameterisation + clamped averaging-rule knots + analytic endpoint CPs (P&T §9.4 Eqs 9.26-9.29); interior CPs by least-squares collocation; 22 hermetic tests (straight-line residual<1e-9, circle-arc residual<1e-6, G2 constraints<1e-9, G1 constraints<1e-9); LLM tool nurbs_fit_curve_g2. HONEST: chord-length only — centripetal parameterisation (Lee 1989) may give better results for high-aspect-ratio or irregularly-spaced data; curvature vectors are parametric C''(t), NOT Frenet kappa*n_hat — use adjacent curve's C''(t_join) for blending. Refs: Piegl & Tiller §9.4; Farin CAGD Ch 10.

features.feature: B-rep face planarity check (SVD best-fit plane)

competitor.note: SOLIDWORKS IFaceFeature.GetPlanarFaceData() returns plane normal + origin for planar faces; non-planar faces not classified

kerf.note: check_face_planarity: uniform UV-grid sampling + SVD orthogonal regression (Pratt 1987 §3; Eberly §6.6); returns is_planar, plane_origin, plane_normal, max_deviation, planarity_score (0→flat, higher→curved); default tolerance=1e-4×bbox_diag; LLM tool brep_check_face_planarity. Useful for sheet-metal flat-pattern feasibility, simple-face fast-rendering, planar-face cooling-channel routing. CAVEAT: v1 uses full UV domain — inner trim loops not excluded.

features.feature: B-rep face developability check (Gaussian curvature K=0 test)

competitor.note: SOLIDWORKS has no direct developability API; sheet-metal module classifies bends as cylindrical/conical but does not expose K=0 sampling or ruled-surface detection for arbitrary NURBS faces

kerf.note: check_face_developable: N×N UV-grid sampling + Gaussian curvature K=κ_1·κ_2 via shape operator (do Carmo §3.6; Pottmann-Wallner §4); returns is_developable (max|K|<tol), max_abs_K, mean_abs_K, ruled_direction_if_any (kappa_1/kappa_2 identically zero); LLM tool brep_check_face_developable. Oracles: cylinder K=0→True; sphere K=1/R²→False; cone K=0→True; torus K varies→False; plane K=0→True. CAVEAT: sampling-based lower bound on max|K|.
