competitor.note: Change management available through PDM/PLM integration (Fusion Manage / Autodesk Vault); no standalone change-impact graph propagation in base Fusion

kerf.note: Change-impact analyzer + co-change suggestions (PROSTEP-iViP). BFS propagation through BOM + assembly + requirements graph; impact levels high/medium/low; rework cost estimate; mates-interface co-change heuristics. LLM tools: plm_change_impact, plm_propose_co_changes.

features.feature: Where-Used Analysis — inverse BOM traversal (PROSTEP-iViP SIG §5.2)

competitor.note: Where-used available only through full PDM/PLM add-ons (e.g. SOLIDWORKS PDM, Fusion Manage, 3DExperience); not available in base modeller.

kerf.note: Where-Used Analysis: reverse-traverses the assembly hierarchy from a target part. Returns all parent assemblies with occurrence multiplicity and depth (depth=1 immediate parent, depth=2 grandparent, …). Cycle detection with cap at depth=20. LLM tool: plm_where_used.

features.feature: Component Where-Used Report — flat BOM inverse traversal with qty and level (ISO 10303-44 + APICS 'where-used')

competitor.note: Component where-used available only through full PDM/PLM add-ons; not natively available in base modeller from flat BOM input.

kerf.note: Given a flat list of BomRelationship(parent_pn, child_pn, qty), BFS upward from queried component to root assemblies. WhereUsedReport: component_pn, num_unique_parents, num_total_usages, max_depth, entries (WhereUsedEntry: parent_pn, parent_name, qty, level). Qty aggregation: multiple rows for same (parent, child) summed. Diamond BOM: shallowest BFS level wins. Cycle detection raises ValueError. Honest-flagged: in-memory traversal — no DB pagination, live PDM sync, effectivity filtering, or path-multiplied extended-qty. LLM tool: plm_component_whereused. 17 tests.

features.feature: Document Version Diff — controlled document delta (ISO 10303-44 §5.2 + Borst-Lahti §6.3)

competitor.note: No built-in document revision diff with field-level criticality classification; requires external PLM/PDM or custom scripting.

kerf.note: Field-level diff for BOM, drawing metadata, or any JSON-like doc revision: added, removed, modified (field-level criticality: engineering vs administrative), renamed (>=80% Jaccard heuristic). 100-item diff < 1 ms. LLM tool: plm_document_version_diff.

features.feature: Multi-Cavity Tool Effectivity — per-cavity insert revision tracking (PROSTEP-iViP SIG §6)

competitor.note: No built-in per-cavity insert effectivity tracking; multi-cavity tooling managed through external tool databases (DataM, ToolMonitor) or custom ERP; not natively modelled in base CAD/PLM.

kerf.note: Per-cavity insert revision state for multi-cavity injection molds: each cavity slot carries an insert history with date-effectivity windows. Query (tool, date) → [(cavity_id, revision), …]. Cavity-3 mid-run swap: query 2026-05-01 → [(1,R5),(2,R5),(3,R6),(4,R5)]; query 2026-03-15 → all R5. Honest flag: no insert wear / change-out queuing modelled. LLM tool: plm_query_multi_cavity.

features.feature: ECN Cascading Impact Analysis — BFS upward BOM traversal, drawings, work orders, heuristic cost (ISO 10007 §6 + APICS 'ECN')

competitor.note: Most CAD/PLM tools do not offer automated ECN cascading impact analysis from a flat BOM input; typically requires full PLM suite (Windchill, Teamcenter, Enovia).

kerf.note: analyze_ecn_impact: BFS upward from all ECN-affected components; deduped parent set; drawings + work-order counts; heuristic cost estimate (parents×$50 + drawings×$150 + WOs×$200); ISO 10007 §6.3.2 class assignment (Class_I_immediate / Class_II_rev / Class_III_drawing_only). Honest-flagged: cost is heuristic — real ECN costs vary widely; use for prioritisation only. LLM tool: plm_analyze_ecn_impact. 23 tests.

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels can classify rational quadratic NURBS as conics (degree-2 gate) but do not expose a general algebraic discriminant-based classifier for arbitrary-degree curves or raw point sets

kerf.note: detect_conic_type(curve_or_points, num_samples=100, residual_threshold_mm=0.01) -> ConicDetectResult(conic_type, conic_coefficients, eccentricity, rms_residual_mm, honest_caveat); fits general conic Ax²+Bxy+Cy²+Dx+Ey+F=0 via SVD null-space (Pratt 1987 + Fitzgibbon-Pilu-Fisher 1999); classifies by discriminant B²−4AC: circle|ellipse|parabola|hyperbola|line|free_form; eccentricity from 3×3 homogeneous matrix eigendecomposition; LLM tool nurbs_detect_conic_type. HONEST: algebraic fit is susceptible to noise bias — for production-quality circle detection use Pratt/Taubin (curve_circle_fit.py); parabola discrimination at discriminant boundary is fragile under noise.

features.name: Degree-5 NURBS curve fit with G2 curvature-continuous end conditions (P&T §9.4)

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels provide G1 (tangent) end-condition interpolation; G2 (curvature) endpoint matching for freeform curve fitting is less common and typically requires accessing lower-level knot/CP construction APIs

kerf.note: fit_curve_g2(spec: G2FitSpec, degree=5) -> G2FitResult; degree-5 B-spline fit to 3-D data points with G2 (curvature-continuous) end conditions; prescribe start_tangent_xyz, start_curvature_xyz, end_tangent_xyz, end_curvature_xyz; chord-length parameterisation + clamped averaging-rule knots + analytic endpoint CPs (P&T §9.4 Eqs 9.26-9.29); interior CPs by least-squares collocation; 22 hermetic tests (straight-line residual<1e-9, circle-arc residual<1e-6, G2 constraints<1e-9, G1 constraints<1e-9); LLM tool nurbs_fit_curve_g2. HONEST: chord-length only — centripetal parameterisation (Lee 1989) may give better results for high-aspect-ratio or irregularly-spaced data; curvature vectors are parametric C''(t), NOT Frenet kappa*n_hat — use adjacent curve's C''(t_join) for blending. Refs: Piegl & Tiller §9.4; Farin CAGD Ch 10.

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels provide G1 (tangent) end-condition interpolation; G2 (curvature) endpoint matching for freeform curve fitting is less common and typically requires accessing lower-level knot/CP construction APIs

kerf.note: fit_curve_g2(spec: G2FitSpec, degree=5) -> G2FitResult; degree-5 B-spline fit to 3-D data points with G2 (curvature-continuous) end conditions; prescribe start_tangent_xyz, start_curvature_xyz, end_tangent_xyz, end_curvature_xyz; chord-length parameterisation + clamped averaging-rule knots + analytic endpoint CPs (P&T §9.4 Eqs 9.26-9.29); interior CPs by least-squares collocation; 22 hermetic tests (straight-line residual<1e-9, circle-arc residual<1e-6, G2 constraints<1e-9, G1 constraints<1e-9); LLM tool nurbs_fit_curve_g2. HONEST: chord-length only — centripetal parameterisation (Lee 1989) may give better results for high-aspect-ratio or irregularly-spaced data; curvature vectors are parametric C''(t), NOT Frenet kappa*n_hat — use adjacent curve's C''(t_join) for blending. Refs: Piegl & Tiller §9.4; Farin CAGD Ch 10.

kerf.note: Chat-native: describe box dimensions in plain language, Kerf generates the dieline

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels can classify rational quadratic NURBS as conics (degree-2 gate) but do not expose a general algebraic discriminant-based classifier for arbitrary-degree curves or raw point sets

kerf.note: detect_conic_type(curve_or_points, num_samples=100, residual_threshold_mm=0.01) -> ConicDetectResult(conic_type, conic_coefficients, eccentricity, rms_residual_mm, honest_caveat); fits general conic Ax²+Bxy+Cy²+Dx+Ey+F=0 via SVD null-space (Pratt 1987 + Fitzgibbon-Pilu-Fisher 1999); classifies by discriminant B²−4AC: circle|ellipse|parabola|hyperbola|line|free_form; eccentricity from 3×3 homogeneous matrix eigendecomposition; LLM tool nurbs_detect_conic_type. HONEST: algebraic fit is susceptible to noise bias — for production-quality circle detection use Pratt/Taubin (curve_circle_fit.py); parabola discrimination at discriminant boundary is fragile under noise.

competitor.note: Change management available through PDM/PLM integration (Fusion Manage / Autodesk Vault); no standalone change-impact graph propagation in base Fusion

kerf.note: Change-impact analyzer + co-change suggestions (PROSTEP-iViP). BFS propagation through BOM + assembly + requirements graph; impact levels high/medium/low; rework cost estimate; mates-interface co-change heuristics. LLM tools: plm_change_impact, plm_propose_co_changes.

features.feature: Where-Used Analysis — inverse BOM traversal (PROSTEP-iViP SIG §5.2)

competitor.note: Where-used available only through full PDM/PLM add-ons (e.g. SOLIDWORKS PDM, Fusion Manage, 3DExperience); not available in base modeller.

kerf.note: Where-Used Analysis: reverse-traverses the assembly hierarchy from a target part. Returns all parent assemblies with occurrence multiplicity and depth (depth=1 immediate parent, depth=2 grandparent, …). Cycle detection with cap at depth=20. LLM tool: plm_where_used.

features.feature: Component Where-Used Report — flat BOM inverse traversal with qty and level (ISO 10303-44 + APICS 'where-used')

competitor.note: Component where-used available only through full PDM/PLM add-ons; not natively available in base modeller from flat BOM input.

kerf.note: Given a flat list of BomRelationship(parent_pn, child_pn, qty), BFS upward from queried component to root assemblies. WhereUsedReport: component_pn, num_unique_parents, num_total_usages, max_depth, entries (WhereUsedEntry: parent_pn, parent_name, qty, level). Qty aggregation: multiple rows for same (parent, child) summed. Diamond BOM: shallowest BFS level wins. Cycle detection raises ValueError. Honest-flagged: in-memory traversal — no DB pagination, live PDM sync, effectivity filtering, or path-multiplied extended-qty. LLM tool: plm_component_whereused. 17 tests.

features.feature: Document Version Diff — controlled document delta (ISO 10303-44 §5.2 + Borst-Lahti §6.3)

competitor.note: No built-in document revision diff with field-level criticality classification; requires external PLM/PDM or custom scripting.

kerf.note: Field-level diff for BOM, drawing metadata, or any JSON-like doc revision: added, removed, modified (field-level criticality: engineering vs administrative), renamed (>=80% Jaccard heuristic). 100-item diff < 1 ms. LLM tool: plm_document_version_diff.

features.feature: Multi-Cavity Tool Effectivity — per-cavity insert revision tracking (PROSTEP-iViP SIG §6)

competitor.note: No built-in per-cavity insert effectivity tracking; multi-cavity tooling managed through external tool databases (DataM, ToolMonitor) or custom ERP; not natively modelled in base CAD/PLM.

kerf.note: Per-cavity insert revision state for multi-cavity injection molds: each cavity slot carries an insert history with date-effectivity windows. Query (tool, date) → [(cavity_id, revision), …]. Cavity-3 mid-run swap: query 2026-05-01 → [(1,R5),(2,R5),(3,R6),(4,R5)]; query 2026-03-15 → all R5. Honest flag: no insert wear / change-out queuing modelled. LLM tool: plm_query_multi_cavity.

features.feature: ECN Cascading Impact Analysis — BFS upward BOM traversal, drawings, work orders, heuristic cost (ISO 10007 §6 + APICS 'ECN')

competitor.note: Most CAD/PLM tools do not offer automated ECN cascading impact analysis from a flat BOM input; typically requires full PLM suite (Windchill, Teamcenter, Enovia).

kerf.note: analyze_ecn_impact: BFS upward from all ECN-affected components; deduped parent set; drawings + work-order counts; heuristic cost estimate (parents×$50 + drawings×$150 + WOs×$200); ISO 10007 §6.3.2 class assignment (Class_I_immediate / Class_II_rev / Class_III_drawing_only). Honest-flagged: cost is heuristic — real ECN costs vary widely; use for prioritisation only. LLM tool: plm_analyze_ecn_impact. 23 tests.

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels can classify rational quadratic NURBS as conics (degree-2 gate) but do not expose a general algebraic discriminant-based classifier for arbitrary-degree curves or raw point sets

kerf.note: detect_conic_type(curve_or_points, num_samples=100, residual_threshold_mm=0.01) -> ConicDetectResult(conic_type, conic_coefficients, eccentricity, rms_residual_mm, honest_caveat); fits general conic Ax²+Bxy+Cy²+Dx+Ey+F=0 via SVD null-space (Pratt 1987 + Fitzgibbon-Pilu-Fisher 1999); classifies by discriminant B²−4AC: circle|ellipse|parabola|hyperbola|line|free_form; eccentricity from 3×3 homogeneous matrix eigendecomposition; LLM tool nurbs_detect_conic_type. HONEST: algebraic fit is susceptible to noise bias — for production-quality circle detection use Pratt/Taubin (curve_circle_fit.py); parabola discrimination at discriminant boundary is fragile under noise.

features.name: Degree-5 NURBS curve fit with G2 curvature-continuous end conditions (P&T §9.4)

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels provide G1 (tangent) end-condition interpolation; G2 (curvature) endpoint matching for freeform curve fitting is less common and typically requires accessing lower-level knot/CP construction APIs

kerf.note: fit_curve_g2(spec: G2FitSpec, degree=5) -> G2FitResult; degree-5 B-spline fit to 3-D data points with G2 (curvature-continuous) end conditions; prescribe start_tangent_xyz, start_curvature_xyz, end_tangent_xyz, end_curvature_xyz; chord-length parameterisation + clamped averaging-rule knots + analytic endpoint CPs (P&T §9.4 Eqs 9.26-9.29); interior CPs by least-squares collocation; 22 hermetic tests (straight-line residual<1e-9, circle-arc residual<1e-6, G2 constraints<1e-9, G1 constraints<1e-9); LLM tool nurbs_fit_curve_g2. HONEST: chord-length only — centripetal parameterisation (Lee 1989) may give better results for high-aspect-ratio or irregularly-spaced data; curvature vectors are parametric C''(t), NOT Frenet kappa*n_hat — use adjacent curve's C''(t_join) for blending. Refs: Piegl & Tiller §9.4; Farin CAGD Ch 10.

items.hero_tagline: Integrated mold CAD/CAM from quote to shop floor — versus an open-core alternative that adds Moldflow fill simulation and multi-domain engineering.

competitor.note: Cimatron 2026 introduces integrated injection simulation; wall thickness, weld lines, air traps

kerf.note: Hele-Shaw front tracking + weld-line + air-trap detection (backend)

competitor.note: Industry's fastest parting and cavity design; undercut detection; split surface generation

kerf.note: Parting line data model (closed 3-D loop); flat and ruled parting surface generation; draft-angle check; moldability validation; no interactive curve-extraction from NURBS solid

competitor.note: Load complete mold base plate sets from commercial catalogues (DME, HASCO, Futaba) in minutes

kerf.note: No mold base library. A parametric DME/HASCO plate-dimension table with 3D parametric solid generation requires OCCT CAD kernel integration (kerf-cad-core wave 2) — not tractable in kerf-mold alone.

competitor.note: Standard and conformal cooling channel design; interference detection against cavities and ejectors

kerf.note: Cooling circuit thermal analysis: Re/Nu/HTC (Dittus-Boelter), coolant temp rise, Janeschitz-Kriegl cooling time; series and parallel layouts. Geometric conflict detection (Menges 2001 §6.5): CHANNEL_SPACING (2x-diam rule), CHANNEL_EJECTOR, WALL_CLEARANCE, MOLD_BOUNDS via segment-segment min-dist (Ericson 2005 §5.1.9); severity 1-5 scale; LLM tool `mold_verify_cooling_channels`. Hydraulic pressure-drop (Beaumont 2007 §11.2; White §6.7): Darcy-Weisbach major losses + K-factor minor losses (elbow_90=0.9, elbow_45=0.4, tee_thru=0.6, tee_branch=1.8); Blasius turbulent f; chiller pump head check with 25% margin; LLM tool `mold_compute_cooling_pressure_drop`. Turbulent-Re check (Beaumont 2007 §11; White §8.1): verify Re≥10000 for fully-turbulent flow and Dittus-Boelter applicability; flag laminar (Re<2300) and transitional zones; compute min recommended flow rate to reach Re=10000; LLM tool `mold_check_turbulent_re`. HONEST: single-phase incompressible only — no two-phase/boiling, no conformal toolpaths; Re check does NOT compute wall heat flux, Nu/HTC, polymer-side boundary layer, or mold-steel thermal resistance.

competitor.note: Automated ejector pin placement with interference detection against cooling channels and mold features

kerf.note: Ejector pin layout planner (Yu-Fan 2003 §10 + SPI/ANSI B151.1): regular-grid placement with 20 mm default spacing; SPI standard diameters (3/16"–1/2" series); dedicated feature pins for bosses and ribs; force distribution (Menges §7.2 side-wall friction model); conflict detection against cooling channels and rib bodies; warpage risk from force non-uniformity (4×4 region grid); LLM tools: mold_plan_ejector_pins + mold_pin_conflicts

competitor.note: Ejector stroke adequacy and pin deflection check integrated in mold tooling workspace

kerf.note: Ejector stroke verifier (Beaumont 2007 §9 + Menges 2001 §7.4): (1) stroke adequacy — required_stroke (part_depth + safety_margin) ≤ machine_stroke; (2) force capacity — pin_count × max_force ≥ ejection_force; (3) pin deflection — δ=F·L³/(3·E·I) ≤ 0.05mm allowable (Euler-Bernoulli cantilever, Beaumont §9.3); (4) knockout bar contact — plate thickness ≥ bar diameter (Beaumont §9.5); LLM tool `mold_verify_ejector_stroke`; static load only, honest-flagged for dynamic/fatigue scope; 33 oracle tests

features.feature: Ejector pin buckling-force check (Euler critical load)

competitor.note: Cimatron's ejector design module sizes pins geometrically from cavity depth/part area but does not expose an explicit Euler buckling-force calculation or demand/capacity ratio check

kerf.note: F_cr = π²·E·I/(K·L)² (Roark's 9e §15.2 + SPI/ANSI B151.1); E=200 GPa all tool-steel grades (M2/H13/S7/D2); I=π·d⁴/64; K=1.0 pinned-pinned (SPI default) / 0.5 fixed-fixed / 2.0 cantilever; DCR=required/F_cr; recommends smallest SPI-standard diameter with F_cr ≥ 1.1×required; short-column warning when K·L/d < 30 (Johnson formula flag); LLM tool `mold_compute_ejector_pin_push`; 45 oracle tests. HONEST: Euler non-conservative for short stout pins; bushing friction and pin eccentricity NOT modelled

competitor.note: Cimatron provides ejector-plate force output only as part of full fill simulation (Moldflow coupling); no standalone analytical formula tool

kerf.note: Demolding force checker (Beaumont 2007 §9.3 + Menges 2001 §7.4 + Table 7.6): cavity-ejection formula F = μ·σ_h·A·cosα/(cosα + μ·sinα); shrinkage stress σ_h from Menges Table 7.6 (ABS=4.0, PC=3.0, PP=5.0, PA66=3.5, POM=4.5 MPa); friction μ from SPI finish class (SPI_A1=0.15 polished … SPI_D1=0.40 textured); ejector pin count = ceil(F / single_pin_capacity_N); LLM tool `mold_compute_demold_force`; 28 oracle tests; honest-flagged: empirical formula only — ignores chemical adhesion (resin stick, relevant for PC/POM on polished steel), undercut/side-action geometry, dynamic ejection force, and pin fatigue; confirm by mold-trial load-cell measurement

features.feature: Mold vent depth check (parting-line air-escape gap)

competitor.note: Cimatron mold wizard provides vent depth guidelines per material class; no automated per-polymer compliance check or depth classifier

kerf.note: Vent depth checker (Beaumont 2007 §8.3 Table 8.2 + Menges 2001 §6.4 Table 6.7): polymer-specific recommended ranges — ABS 0.025–0.038 mm, PC 0.013–0.025 mm, PP 0.025–0.050 mm, PA66 0.013–0.025 mm, POM 0.013–0.025 mm, PMMA 0.025–0.038 mm, PE 0.038–0.075 mm; depth_class: too_shallow|correct|too_deep|flash_risk (>25% above max); unknown polymers get fallback [0.013–0.038 mm] with caveat; VentSpec dataclass (polymer_grade, proposed_depth_mm, land_length_mm=1.5, vent_width_mm=6.0); LLM tool ; 35 oracle tests; honest-flagged: empirical handbook ranges only — actual safe depth depends on resin batch viscosity, melt temp, injection speed, mold surface finish; filled/FR grades may flash within the correct band; confirm by mold trial + visual flash inspection

competitor.note: Cimatron provides runner system design tools but no automated per-junction cold-slug well sizing compliance check

kerf.note: Cold-slug well checker (Beaumont 2007 §6.7 + Menges 2001 §6.5): verify slug-well diameter = 1.5× and depth = 2.0× runner diameter at each junction; ±20% tolerance band (1.2×–1.8× dia, 1.6×–2.4× depth); RunnerJunctionSpec dataclass (junction_id, runner_diameter_mm, slug_well_diameter_mm, slug_well_depth_mm, polymer_grade); ColdSlugReport with per-junction slug_well_compliant, recommended dims, reason string; LLM tool `mold_check_cold_slug_design`; 32 oracle tests; honest-flagged: geometric rule-of-thumb only — actual cold-slug capture depends on melt temperature, injection speed, and resin freeze-off time; fast-crystallising/filled grades may need larger wells; confirm by mold trial + gate-area visual inspection

features.feature: Vent slot layout (count, width, spacing for air displacement)

competitor.note: Cimatron mold wizard provides manual vent placement tools but no automated vent-slot count and spacing calculation from cavity volume and injection speed

kerf.note: Vent slot layout (Beaumont 2007 §8.5 + Menges 2001 §6.4): propose slot count, width, and spacing from cavity volume, parting-line perimeter, polymer grade, and injection speed; Beaumont 0.5%-projected-area rule (total_vent_area ≥ 0.005 × volume_mm³/3mm wall); speed scaling: vent_area_factor = max(1.0, speed/50 cm³/s); slot width = 6 mm standard; polymer-specific vent depth midpoint for per-slot area sizing; minimum 4 slots; 10 mm minimum steel bridge between slots; MoldVolumeSpec dataclass (cavity_volume_cm3, parting_line_perimeter_mm, polymer_grade, injection_speed_cm3_s); VentSlotLayoutReport dataclass (num_vent_slots, vent_slot_width_mm, vent_slot_spacing_mm, total_vent_width_mm, air_displacement_rate_cm3_s, adequate, honest_caveat); LLM tool `mold_generate_vent_slot_layout`; 47 oracle tests; honest-flagged: heuristic rule from production experience — NOT first-principles fluid dynamics; wall thickness assumed 3 mm for area estimation; uniform spacing only — use mold_optimize_vent_placement for last-to-fill location; actual fill simulation needs Moldflow/Moldex3D/SigmaSoft

competitor.note: Draft angle analysis per face with colour-mapped pass/fail display; integrated in Cimatron mold tooling workspace

kerf.note: Per-face draft-angle validation: arcsin(|n̂·pull̂|) angle; SPI finish grades A1–D3 minimum lookup (Menges 2001 §3.4 + Beaumont 2007 §4); region-aware minimums (outer 0.5°, inner 1.0°, rib 1.0°, boss 0.5°); degenerate-normal flagging; LLM tool `mold_validate_draft`; 38 oracle tests; v1 honest-flag: undercut detection out of scope

competitor.note: Automated gate-location analysis: fill-balance, pressure, weld-line prediction integrated in mold workspace

kerf.note: Gate placement optimiser (Beaumont 2007 §7 + Menges 2001 §6.6): samples candidates on all bbox faces (top-centre, edge, side, bottom, multi-gate seeds); scores by Euclidean max-flow-length + fill-balance std-dev (0.6/0.4 weighted composite); functional-face + avoid-zone constraints; soft penalty for underside gates; multi-gate suggestion when flow/min-dim > 5×; balance score 0–1; LLM tool `mold_optimize_gate_placement`; 41 oracle tests; honest-flag: geometric heuristic only — not Moldflow/Moldex3D/SigmaSoft

competitor.note: Vent location guidance in mold wizard; no automated geometric optimiser

kerf.note: Air-vent placement optimiser (Beaumont 2007 §8.4 + Table 8.4): last-fill zones (4 farthest-from-gate corners, priority 1), parting-line rib positions (priority 2), sharp-corner pockets (priority 3); material-specific vent depths — ABS 0.025–0.040 mm, PP/PE 0.020–0.030 mm, POM/PA/PBT/PET 0.013–0.020 mm (crystalline), LCP/PPS 0.010–0.015 mm; land length 0.8 mm; avoid-zone constraint; LLM tool `mold_optimize_vent_placement`; 72 oracle tests; honest-flag: geometric heuristic only — not Moldflow/Moldex3D/SigmaSoft

competitor.note: Cimatron 2026 injection simulation includes wall-thickness and fill analysis; L/T ratio not explicitly surfaced as a standalone metric

kerf.note: L/T ratio checker (Beaumont 2007 §4 Table 4.2 + Menges 2001 §6.2.1): lt_ratio = flow_length_mm / wall_thickness_mm per feature; MATERIAL_LT_LIMITS — ABS=150, PC=220, PP=300, PA66=250, POM=200, PMMA=180; risk: ≤80% limit → safe, 80–100% → caution, >100% → short_shot; recommended_min_thickness = flow_length/(limit×0.85) (15% margin); LLM tool `mold_check_flow_length`; 23 oracle tests; honest-flag: L/T ratio check only — NOT Hagen-Poiseuille pressure-drop simulation or melt-front thermal analysis; use Moldflow/Moldex3D/SigmaSoft for full fill simulation

competitor.note: Cimatron cooling-circuit analysis includes cycle-time estimates; full 3-D thermal solver in separate Moldflow product

kerf.note: Chen-Chiang (1985) 1-D Fourier first-term cooling-time formula (Menges 2001 §7.3.3 + Beaumont 2007 §10.4): t_c = (h²/π²α)·ln[(8/π²)·(T_m−T_w)/(T_e−T_w)]; MATERIAL_THERMAL_DB (Menges 2001 Table 7.3) — ABS α=1.00e-7, PC α=1.50e-7, PP α=0.95e-7, PA66 α=1.40e-7, POM α=0.95e-7, PMMA α=1.13e-7 m²/s; dominant-factor diagnosis (thickness_squared|diffusivity|temp_window); LLM tool `mold_compute_cooling_time_chen_chiang`; 24 oracle tests; honest-flag: 1-D Fourier first-term approximation only — ignores cooling-channel layout and conformal-cooling effects; does NOT model crystallisation latent heat (semi-crystalline PP/PA66/POM times 15–30% longer), thermal contact resistance, or wall-thickness-variation hot spots; use Moldflow/Moldex3D/SigmaSoft for a full cooling-circuit simulation

features.feature: Runner network balance check (Hagen-Poiseuille path resistance)

competitor.note: Cimatron mold runner design includes symmetric runner layout tools; explicit Hagen-Poiseuille path-resistance imbalance percentage not surfaced as a standalone metric

kerf.note: Hagen-Poiseuille path-resistance balance checker (Beaumont 2007 §6.6 + Menges 2001 §6.6.4): R_norm = L/r⁴ per segment (μ cancels in ratios); path resistance summed sprue→gate via parent-pointer walk; max_imbalance_pct = 100×(R_max−R_min)/R_mean; balanced=True iff max_imbalance_pct < 5%; fill_ratio per cavity; LLM tool `mold_check_runner_balance`; 42 oracle tests; honest-flag: geometric resistance only — does NOT model temperature-dependent or shear-thinning viscosity, shear-heating asymmetry in graduated-diameter runners (Beaumont RPI runner), or transient filling inertia; use Moldflow/Moldex3D/SigmaSoft for full rheological fill simulation

competitor.note: Cimatron includes runner diameter sizing tools based on empirical guidelines but does not expose the Beaumont §6.5 W^0.25×√L/3.7 formula explicitly; no polymer-specific viscosity adjustment or cold-runner waste output

kerf.note: Beaumont (2007) §6.5 optimal runner diameter: D=(W^0.25×√L)/3.7 with material-viscosity adjustments (ABS+10%, PC+15%, PP-5%, PA66±0%); cold-runner waste (cylindrical volume × polymer density); fill-pressure proxy (Hagen-Poiseuille K_mat·L/D^4); LLM tool `mold_optimize_runner_diameter`; 28 oracle tests; HONEST: single runner segment only — does NOT optimise filling balance in multi-cavity molds; empirical formula only (not Hele-Shaw/Cross-WLF rheological simulation); fill-pressure is order-of-magnitude proxy; confirm with Moldflow/Moldex3D fill simulation + mold trial. Refs: Beaumont 2007 §6.5; Menges 2001 §6.5

competitor.note: Cimatron and Mastercam include basic warpage-risk guidance but do not expose a quantitative 0–100 heuristic index with weighted sub-scores (wall uniformity, gate location, polymer grade, cooling time, mold temperature) or automated mitigation suggestions; full warpage simulation requires a separate Moldflow/Moldex3D licence

kerf.note: Heuristic warpage-risk index 0–100 (Beaumont 2007 §10 + Menges 2001 §8): wall-uniformity penalty (0–30 pts; 100%=0, 80%=6, 0%=30); gate-location penalty (0–25 pts: centered=3, edge=14, corner=22, unbalanced=25); polymer grade (0–20 pts: PC=5 low, GF-PA66=20 max — Menges 2001 Table 8.2 shrinkage anisotropy); post-eject cooling time (0–15 pts; exponential 15·exp(−t/40)); mold temp deviation from recommended range (0–10 pts; Menges 2001 §5.4 Table 5.3). Risk thresholds: 0–24 low / 25–49 medium / 50–74 high / 75–100 severe. Prioritised mitigation suggestions per dominant factor. LLM tool `mold_compute_warpage_index`; 39 oracle tests. HONEST: screening tool only (±15 pts uncertainty typical) — real warpage prediction requires Moldflow/Moldex3D/SigmaSoft FEM; orientation-dependent shrinkage tensors, part geometry, packing pressure, injection speed, hold time, and residual-stress relaxation are NOT modelled. Refs: Beaumont 2007 §10; Menges 2001 §8

competitor.note: Hybrid electrode design (surfaces + solids); spark gap definition; auto blank-cutting from holder shapes

kerf.note: No electrode design. EDM electrode solid modelling requires full parametric 3D CAD (OCCT kernel, kerf-cad-core wave 2). Spark-gap compensation can be handled as offset surface; electrode blanking needs full solid Boolean ops.

competitor.note: 2.5-axis to 5-axis milling and drilling; material removal simulator; gouge/collision detection

kerf.note: 5-axis engine (backend); no UI; 3-axis CAMView wired in browser

competitor.note: Cimatron 2026 introduces integrated Wire EDM for 2-axis and 4-axis CNC programming

kerf.note: No wire EDM programming. Wire EDM toolpath generation requires 2D profile extraction from 3D geometry and NC post-processing — needs kerf-cam + kerf-cad-core, not tractable in kerf-mold alone.

competitor.note: Draft angle and direction analysis; body integrity and wall thickness checks

kerf.note: Draft angle per face: signed draft_deg = asin(n·pull_hat); undercut detection; wall-thickness uniformity check; parting-surface planarity check vs pull direction — all in check_moldability

features.feature: Injection-speed envelope from MFR (jetting / sink / gate freeze)

competitor.note: No MFR-driven injection-speed recommendation in Cimatron; requires separate Moldflow/Sigmasoft analysis

kerf.note: mold_check_melt_flow_ratio: from polymer MFR (ASTM D1238 g/10 min), wall thickness, and gate type → recommended injection-speed envelope (mm/s) + qualitative jetting / sink-mark / gate-freeze risk flags. References: Beaumont 2007 §4 + Menges 2001 §6.2. HONEST: heuristic envelope only; MFR is a single-point viscosity proxy; real speed tuning needs full viscosity curve + on-tool DOE.

competitor.note: No automated sprue bushing / machine nozzle seat-radius and orifice compliance check in Cimatron; requires manual catalogue lookup

kerf.note: mold_check_sprue_bushing_match: verify sprue bushing seat radius R = nozzle_r + 0.5–1.0 mm; sprue orifice O = nozzle_O + 0.5–1.0 mm; taper 1.5–3.0°/side (DME standard §3.2). Dataclasses SprueBushingSpec + MachineNozzleSpec; SprueMatchReport (R_mismatch_mm, R_compliant, O_mismatch_mm, O_compliant, taper_compliant, recommendation, honest_caveat). 35 oracle tests. HONEST: standard cold-runner bushings only — hot-runner nozzle seats (DME hot-tip, valve-gate, edge-gate) follow different design rules and are NOT covered. References: Beaumont 2007 §6.4 + DME Catalogue §3.2.

competitor.note: Motion analysis and collision detection for mold assembly verification (slides, lifters, ejectors)

kerf.note: Assembly clash detection backend (OBB-SAT + BVH); no mold-specific motion/collision sequence

competitor.note: Integrated from quote to design and manufacturing in a single CAD/CAM interface

kerf.note: Should-cost engine + BOM (backend); no mold-specific quoting workflow

competitor.note: No LLM interface in Cimatron as of May 2026

kerf.note: Chat-native editing; Moldflow results describable in plain language

features.feature: Gate vestige estimation and cosmetic-class compliance

competitor.note: Cimatron provides gate geometry design tools but no automatic vestige height prediction or cosmetic-class compliance report

kerf.note: Empirical vestige estimates per Beaumont 2007 §7.6 Table 7.4 for edge, tunnel, submarine, hot_tip, pin_point, fan, film gates; checks against A1/A2/A3/B/C cosmetic classes (SPI/Beaumont); removal method guidance per gate type. Honest caveat: estimates only — actual vestige depends on degating tool sharpness, melt temperature, and resin grade ductility; confirm by molding trial for A1/A2 surfaces.

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels can classify rational quadratic NURBS as conics (degree-2 gate) but do not expose a general algebraic discriminant-based classifier for arbitrary-degree curves or raw point sets

kerf.note: detect_conic_type(curve_or_points, num_samples=100, residual_threshold_mm=0.01) -> ConicDetectResult(conic_type, conic_coefficients, eccentricity, rms_residual_mm, honest_caveat); fits general conic Ax²+Bxy+Cy²+Dx+Ey+F=0 via SVD null-space (Pratt 1987 + Fitzgibbon-Pilu-Fisher 1999); classifies by discriminant B²−4AC: circle|ellipse|parabola|hyperbola|line|free_form; eccentricity from 3×3 homogeneous matrix eigendecomposition; LLM tool nurbs_detect_conic_type. HONEST: algebraic fit is susceptible to noise bias — for production-quality circle detection use Pratt/Taubin (curve_circle_fit.py); parabola discrimination at discriminant boundary is fragile under noise.

features.name: Degree-5 NURBS curve fit with G2 curvature-continuous end conditions (P&T §9.4)

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels provide G1 (tangent) end-condition interpolation; G2 (curvature) endpoint matching for freeform curve fitting is less common and typically requires accessing lower-level knot/CP construction APIs

kerf.note: fit_curve_g2(spec: G2FitSpec, degree=5) -> G2FitResult; degree-5 B-spline fit to 3-D data points with G2 (curvature-continuous) end conditions; prescribe start_tangent_xyz, start_curvature_xyz, end_tangent_xyz, end_curvature_xyz; chord-length parameterisation + clamped averaging-rule knots + analytic endpoint CPs (P&T §9.4 Eqs 9.26-9.29); interior CPs by least-squares collocation; 22 hermetic tests (straight-line residual<1e-9, circle-arc residual<1e-6, G2 constraints<1e-9, G1 constraints<1e-9); LLM tool nurbs_fit_curve_g2. HONEST: chord-length only — centripetal parameterisation (Lee 1989) may give better results for high-aspect-ratio or irregularly-spaced data; curvature vectors are parametric C''(t), NOT Frenet kappa*n_hat — use adjacent curve's C''(t_join) for blending. Refs: Piegl & Tiller §9.4; Farin CAGD Ch 10.

features.feature: Where-Used Analysis — inverse BOM traversal (PROSTEP-iViP SIG §5.2)

competitor.note: Where-used available only through full PDM/PLM add-ons (e.g. SOLIDWORKS PDM, Fusion Manage, 3DExperience); not available in base modeller.

kerf.note: Where-Used Analysis: reverse-traverses the assembly hierarchy from a target part. Returns all parent assemblies with occurrence multiplicity and depth (depth=1 immediate parent, depth=2 grandparent, …). Cycle detection with cap at depth=20. LLM tool: plm_where_used.

features.feature: Component Where-Used Report — flat BOM inverse traversal with qty and level (ISO 10303-44 + APICS 'where-used')

competitor.note: Component where-used available only through full PDM/PLM add-ons; not natively available in base modeller from flat BOM input.

kerf.note: Given a flat list of BomRelationship(parent_pn, child_pn, qty), BFS upward from queried component to root assemblies. WhereUsedReport: component_pn, num_unique_parents, num_total_usages, max_depth, entries (WhereUsedEntry: parent_pn, parent_name, qty, level). Qty aggregation: multiple rows for same (parent, child) summed. Diamond BOM: shallowest BFS level wins. Cycle detection raises ValueError. Honest-flagged: in-memory traversal — no DB pagination, live PDM sync, effectivity filtering, or path-multiplied extended-qty. LLM tool: plm_component_whereused. 17 tests.

features.feature: Document Version Diff — controlled document delta (ISO 10303-44 §5.2 + Borst-Lahti §6.3)

competitor.note: No built-in document revision diff with field-level criticality classification; requires external PLM/PDM or custom scripting.

kerf.note: Field-level diff for BOM, drawing metadata, or any JSON-like doc revision: added, removed, modified (field-level criticality: engineering vs administrative), renamed (>=80% Jaccard heuristic). 100-item diff < 1 ms. LLM tool: plm_document_version_diff.

features.feature: Multi-Cavity Tool Effectivity — per-cavity insert revision tracking (PROSTEP-iViP SIG §6)

competitor.note: No built-in per-cavity insert effectivity tracking; multi-cavity tooling managed through external tool databases (DataM, ToolMonitor) or custom ERP; not natively modelled in base CAD/PLM.

kerf.note: Per-cavity insert revision state for multi-cavity injection molds: each cavity slot carries an insert history with date-effectivity windows. Query (tool, date) → [(cavity_id, revision), …]. Cavity-3 mid-run swap: query 2026-05-01 → [(1,R5),(2,R5),(3,R6),(4,R5)]; query 2026-03-15 → all R5. Honest flag: no insert wear / change-out queuing modelled. LLM tool: plm_query_multi_cavity.

features.feature: ECN Cascading Impact Analysis — BFS upward BOM traversal, drawings, work orders, heuristic cost (ISO 10007 §6 + APICS 'ECN')

competitor.note: Most CAD/PLM tools do not offer automated ECN cascading impact analysis from a flat BOM input; typically requires full PLM suite (Windchill, Teamcenter, Enovia).

kerf.note: analyze_ecn_impact: BFS upward from all ECN-affected components; deduped parent set; drawings + work-order counts; heuristic cost estimate (parents×$50 + drawings×$150 + WOs×$200); ISO 10007 §6.3.2 class assignment (Class_I_immediate / Class_II_rev / Class_III_drawing_only). Honest-flagged: cost is heuristic — real ECN costs vary widely; use for prioritisation only. LLM tool: plm_analyze_ecn_impact. 23 tests.

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels can classify rational quadratic NURBS as conics (degree-2 gate) but do not expose a general algebraic discriminant-based classifier for arbitrary-degree curves or raw point sets

kerf.note: detect_conic_type(curve_or_points, num_samples=100, residual_threshold_mm=0.01) -> ConicDetectResult(conic_type, conic_coefficients, eccentricity, rms_residual_mm, honest_caveat); fits general conic Ax²+Bxy+Cy²+Dx+Ey+F=0 via SVD null-space (Pratt 1987 + Fitzgibbon-Pilu-Fisher 1999); classifies by discriminant B²−4AC: circle|ellipse|parabola|hyperbola|line|free_form; eccentricity from 3×3 homogeneous matrix eigendecomposition; LLM tool nurbs_detect_conic_type. HONEST: algebraic fit is susceptible to noise bias — for production-quality circle detection use Pratt/Taubin (curve_circle_fit.py); parabola discrimination at discriminant boundary is fragile under noise.

features.name: Degree-5 NURBS curve fit with G2 curvature-continuous end conditions (P&T §9.4)

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels provide G1 (tangent) end-condition interpolation; G2 (curvature) endpoint matching for freeform curve fitting is less common and typically requires accessing lower-level knot/CP construction APIs

kerf.note: fit_curve_g2(spec: G2FitSpec, degree=5) -> G2FitResult; degree-5 B-spline fit to 3-D data points with G2 (curvature-continuous) end conditions; prescribe start_tangent_xyz, start_curvature_xyz, end_tangent_xyz, end_curvature_xyz; chord-length parameterisation + clamped averaging-rule knots + analytic endpoint CPs (P&T §9.4 Eqs 9.26-9.29); interior CPs by least-squares collocation; 22 hermetic tests (straight-line residual<1e-9, circle-arc residual<1e-6, G2 constraints<1e-9, G1 constraints<1e-9); LLM tool nurbs_fit_curve_g2. HONEST: chord-length only — centripetal parameterisation (Lee 1989) may give better results for high-aspect-ratio or irregularly-spaced data; curvature vectors are parametric C''(t), NOT Frenet kappa*n_hat — use adjacent curve's C''(t_join) for blending. Refs: Piegl & Tiller §9.4; Farin CAGD Ch 10.

competitor.note: Change management available through PDM/PLM integration (Fusion Manage / Autodesk Vault); no standalone change-impact graph propagation in base Fusion

kerf.note: Change-impact analyzer + co-change suggestions (PROSTEP-iViP). BFS propagation through BOM + assembly + requirements graph; impact levels high/medium/low; rework cost estimate; mates-interface co-change heuristics. LLM tools: plm_change_impact, plm_propose_co_changes.

features.feature: Where-Used Analysis — inverse BOM traversal (PROSTEP-iViP SIG §5.2)

competitor.note: Where-used available only through full PDM/PLM add-ons (e.g. SOLIDWORKS PDM, Fusion Manage, 3DExperience); not available in base modeller.

kerf.note: Where-Used Analysis: reverse-traverses the assembly hierarchy from a target part. Returns all parent assemblies with occurrence multiplicity and depth (depth=1 immediate parent, depth=2 grandparent, …). Cycle detection with cap at depth=20. LLM tool: plm_where_used.

features.feature: Component Where-Used Report — flat BOM inverse traversal with qty and level (ISO 10303-44 + APICS 'where-used')

competitor.note: Component where-used available only through full PDM/PLM add-ons; not natively available in base modeller from flat BOM input.

kerf.note: Given a flat list of BomRelationship(parent_pn, child_pn, qty), BFS upward from queried component to root assemblies. WhereUsedReport: component_pn, num_unique_parents, num_total_usages, max_depth, entries (WhereUsedEntry: parent_pn, parent_name, qty, level). Qty aggregation: multiple rows for same (parent, child) summed. Diamond BOM: shallowest BFS level wins. Cycle detection raises ValueError. Honest-flagged: in-memory traversal — no DB pagination, live PDM sync, effectivity filtering, or path-multiplied extended-qty. LLM tool: plm_component_whereused. 17 tests.

features.feature: Document Version Diff — controlled document delta (ISO 10303-44 §5.2 + Borst-Lahti §6.3)

competitor.note: No built-in document revision diff with field-level criticality classification; requires external PLM/PDM or custom scripting.

kerf.note: Field-level diff for BOM, drawing metadata, or any JSON-like doc revision: added, removed, modified (field-level criticality: engineering vs administrative), renamed (>=80% Jaccard heuristic). 100-item diff < 1 ms. LLM tool: plm_document_version_diff.

features.feature: Multi-Cavity Tool Effectivity — per-cavity insert revision tracking (PROSTEP-iViP SIG §6)

competitor.note: No built-in per-cavity insert effectivity tracking; multi-cavity tooling managed through external tool databases (DataM, ToolMonitor) or custom ERP; not natively modelled in base CAD/PLM.

kerf.note: Per-cavity insert revision state for multi-cavity injection molds: each cavity slot carries an insert history with date-effectivity windows. Query (tool, date) → [(cavity_id, revision), …]. Cavity-3 mid-run swap: query 2026-05-01 → [(1,R5),(2,R5),(3,R6),(4,R5)]; query 2026-03-15 → all R5. Honest flag: no insert wear / change-out queuing modelled. LLM tool: plm_query_multi_cavity.

features.feature: ECN Cascading Impact Analysis — BFS upward BOM traversal, drawings, work orders, heuristic cost (ISO 10007 §6 + APICS 'ECN')

competitor.note: Most CAD/PLM tools do not offer automated ECN cascading impact analysis from a flat BOM input; typically requires full PLM suite (Windchill, Teamcenter, Enovia).

kerf.note: analyze_ecn_impact: BFS upward from all ECN-affected components; deduped parent set; drawings + work-order counts; heuristic cost estimate (parents×$50 + drawings×$150 + WOs×$200); ISO 10007 §6.3.2 class assignment (Class_I_immediate / Class_II_rev / Class_III_drawing_only). Honest-flagged: cost is heuristic — real ECN costs vary widely; use for prioritisation only. LLM tool: plm_analyze_ecn_impact. 23 tests.

kerf.note: fit_curve_g2(spec: G2FitSpec, degree=5) -> G2FitResult; degree-5 B-spline fit to 3-D data points with G2 (curvature-continuous) end conditions; prescribe start_tangent_xyz, start_curvature_xyz, end_tangent_xyz, end_curvature_xyz; chord-length parameterisation + clamped averaging-rule knots + analytic endpoint CPs (P&T §9.4 Eqs 9.26-9.29); interior CPs by least-squares collocation; 22 hermetic tests (straight-line residual<1e-9, circle-arc residual<1e-6, G2 constraints<1e-9, G1 constraints<1e-9); LLM tool nurbs_fit_curve_g2. HONEST: chord-length only — centripetal parameterisation (Lee 1989) may give better results for high-aspect-ratio or irregularly-spaced data; curvature vectors are parametric C''(t), NOT Frenet kappa*n_hat — use adjacent curve's C''(t_join) for blending. Refs: Piegl & Tiller §9.4; Farin CAGD Ch 10.

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels can classify rational quadratic NURBS as conics (degree-2 gate) but do not expose a general algebraic discriminant-based classifier for arbitrary-degree curves or raw point sets

kerf.note: detect_conic_type(curve_or_points, num_samples=100, residual_threshold_mm=0.01) -> ConicDetectResult(conic_type, conic_coefficients, eccentricity, rms_residual_mm, honest_caveat); fits general conic Ax²+Bxy+Cy²+Dx+Ey+F=0 via SVD null-space (Pratt 1987 + Fitzgibbon-Pilu-Fisher 1999); classifies by discriminant B²−4AC: circle|ellipse|parabola|hyperbola|line|free_form; eccentricity from 3×3 homogeneous matrix eigendecomposition; LLM tool nurbs_detect_conic_type. HONEST: algebraic fit is susceptible to noise bias — for production-quality circle detection use Pratt/Taubin (curve_circle_fit.py); parabola discrimination at discriminant boundary is fragile under noise.

items.hero_tagline: The gold standard for optical design and tolerancing — versus an open-core CAD with ray tracing, Gaussian beam propagation, physical-optics propagation, and acoustics.

competitor.note: Sequential mode: full paraxial ray tracing; ABCD matrix; system cardinal points

kerf.note: Paraxial ABCD ray transfer matrix (backend)

competitor.note: Full sequential mode: multi-surface, multi-wavelength, apertures, aberrations, merit function optimisation

kerf.note: Sequential paraxial + meridional ray trace through multi-element lens stacks: Welford 1986 §3.3/§5 exact Snell + Newton-Raphson conic intersect; EFL/BFL/FFL; Hecht biconvex BK7 oracle <0.1% error; LLM tool optics_ray_trace_lens_stack. v1: no Seidel coefficients, no polychromatic trace.

competitor.note: Full Seidel aberration coefficients; Zernike fringe; wavefront error maps; MTF

kerf.note: Seidel aberrations S_I..S_V (five Seidel coefficients, Welford 1986 §6.2 / Born & Wolf §5.3); Zernike polynomial wavefront fitting (OPTICS-ZERNIKE-WAVEFRONT-FIT): first 15 Noll-ordered terms (j=1..15, piston through tetrafoil); explicit Z_j(ρ,θ) formulas (Noll 1976 Table 1 orthonormal basis); least-squares fit via numpy.linalg.lstsq; RMS residual in waves; dominant aberration = argmax(|c_j|) for j≥2 (piston excluded); LLM tool optics_fit_zernike_wavefront; 32 hermetic tests. MTF across field: ray-bundle PSF histogram + numpy.fft.rfft; tangential MTF at arbitrary field angles; BK7 biconvex off-axis MTF drop verified; LLM tool optics_mtf_across_field. Piston/tip/tilt/defocus alignment analysis (OPTICS-WAVEFRONT-PISTON-TIP-TILT): extracts Z1..Z4 Noll-ordered Zernike alignment components from sampled W(rho,theta); reports in waves at given wavelength; PistonTipTiltReport dataclass; dominant_misalignment = argmax(|c_j|) for j=1..4; LLM tool optics_analyze_wavefront_alignment; 45 hermetic tests. Honest: circular unit-disk pupil only; alignment analysis only -- corrects rigid-body misalignment (piston, tip, tilt, defocus); does NOT correct higher-order aberrations. Ref: Hecht §11.3; Born & Wolf §9.2; Wyant & Creath (1992); Noll (1976). Honest: Zernike fit is first 15 terms only (unit-disk pupil; no higher-order surface fitting; no wavefront OPD tracing -- sampled W(rho,theta) input only). Ref: Noll (1976) J. Opt. Soc. Am. 66 207; Born & Wolf §9.2; Wyant & Creath (1992).

competitor.note: Full vignetting analysis: pupil shifting, real-ray vignetting curves, relative illumination plot per field point

kerf.note: Chief-ray vignetting (OPTICS-CHIEF-RAY-VIGNETTING): marginal-ray bundle sampled around entrance pupil at each field angle; per-surface clear-aperture clipping check; relative illumination = surviving fraction; cos4(theta) photometric baseline; excess-vignetting ratio RI/cos4; LLM tool optics_compute_vignetting. Welford 1986 §4.5 / Hecht §6.6. Circular symmetric apertures; anamorphic/off-axis stops not modelled. PLUS: vignetting fraction check (OPTICS-VIGNETTING-CHECK): LensClearApertureSpec + VignettingReport dataclasses; paraxial chief-ray displacement Δ=z·tan(θ); exact two-circle intersection area (Weisstein) of pupil disk vs per-surface CA disk; vignetting_pct = (1−A_eff/A_full)×100; limiting_surface_idx identified; LLM tool optics_compute_vignetting_check; 34 hermetic tests. Honest: paraxial only; circular CAs only; diffraction-induced vignetting NOT modelled; chromatic pupil walk NOT modelled. Ref: Welford 1986 §3.7; Hecht §5.7.

features.feature: Spot diagrams and pupil illumination maps

competitor.note: Full spot diagram with wavelength weighting, wavefront map, OPD fan; exit-pupil illumination map

kerf.note: Spot diagrams + pupil illumination maps (OPTICS-PUPIL-DIAGRAM): uniform pupil-grid ray trace (Welford 1986 §8.2); per-field (x,y) intercepts at image plane; 2-D RMS and meridional y-only RMS spot radius; max ray distance from chief ray; exit-pupil illumination map (surviving pupil coords); paraxial exit-pupil position estimate (Welford 1986 §3.5 / Hecht §5.7); stigmatic stack RMS < 1e-6 mm; BK7 biconvex at 14deg y-RMS > 3x on-axis (coma verified); Seidel S_II cross-check; LLM tool optics_pupil_diagram. PLUS: OPTICS-SPOT-DIAGRAM — dedicated spot-diagram module: SpotDiagramResult dataclass (image_points_xy, rms_radius_mm, encircled_80pct_radius_mm, centroid_xy, svg_diagram, honest_caveat); geometric EE80 radius (Hecht §6.3); Airy-disk reference ring (Hecht §10.2); SVG with RMS/EE80/Airy rings + centroid + scale bar; LLM tool optics_compute_spot_diagram; 24 hermetic tests. Honest: monochromatic only; sagittal x = first-order paraxial estimate; no chromatic dispersion; single wavelength; stop at first surface. Ref: Hecht (2017) §6.3, §10.2; Welford (1986) §5.2-5.3, §6, §8.2.

features.feature: Aspheric surface sagitta z(r) + arrow chart (ISO 10110-12)

competitor.note: Aspheric surface editor with sag profile display and ISO 10110-12 export; supports conic, even asphere, Q-polynomial (Forbes), XY polynomial, and freeform (Zernike) surface types

kerf.note: Sagitta arrow chart (OPTICS-SAGITTA-ARROW-CHART): conic + even-power aspheric surface z(r) = c*r^2/(1+sqrt(1-(1+k)*c^2*r^2)) + sum(a_i*r^(2i+4)) sampled across clear aperture; SVG chart with radius/sagitta axes + slope arrow markers at every 5th sample (dz/dr); AsphericSurfaceSpec dataclass; SagittaArrowChartResult (sagitta_samples, max_sagitta_mm, conic_only_sagitta_mm, aspheric_contribution_mm, svg_chart, honest_caveat); sphere R=50 r=10 oracle z~1.0102 verified <1e-6; paraboloid k=-1 z=r^2/(2R) exact; hemisphere z=R at edge; domain-violation guard; LLM tool optics_compute_sagitta_arrow_chart; 28 hermetic tests. Honest: conic + even-power polynomial asphere ONLY (ISO 10110-12 S6.2); no Zernike / freeform / Q-polynomial / XY-polynomial / off-axis / tilted surfaces. Ref: Welford (1986) S3.3; ISO 10110-12:2019.

features.feature: Through-focus / defocus curve (RMS spot vs. focus shift)

competitor.note: Through-focus analysis: RMS spot size, MTF, and Strehl ratio vs. defocus; polychromatic and per-wavelength; astigmatic focus splitting

kerf.note: Through-focus defocus curve (OPTICS-DEFOCUS-CURVE): per-Dz meridional RMS spot-size via exact Snell + Newton-Raphson conic intersect (Welford 1986 §11.5 / Hecht §6.5); best-focus shift quantified; parabolic growth verified for paraxial singlet (min at Dz=0); spherical-aberration-shifted minimum verified for full-aperture BK7 singlet (Dz approx -0.5 mm); field-angle dependency; LLM tool optics_defocus_curve; 33 hermetic tests. Honest: monochromatic only; meridional (tangential) RMS only; astigmatic sagittal/tangential splitting not implemented.

competitor.note: Distortion analysis: grid distortion map, percent distortion vs field, barrel/pincushion/TV distortion display; polychromatic

kerf.note: Geometric distortion map (OPTICS-DISTORTION-MAP): chief-ray tangential distortion D=(y_actual-y_paraxial)/|y_paraxial|x100 vs field angle (Hecht SS5.6 / Welford 1986 SS6.3); barrel (D<0) / pincushion (D>0) / mixed classification; Seidel S_V third-order cross-check (Welford SS6.3); BK7 biconvex singlet >0.5% barrel distortion at 20 deg field verified; LLM tool optics_distortion_map; 30 hermetic tests. Honest: monochromatic only; tangential (meridional) distortion only; stop assumed at first surface.

features.feature: Coma aberration metrics (comatic flare length, Seidel S_II comparison)

competitor.note: Coma analysis: tangential and sagittal coma vs field angle; transverse ray aberration plots; Seidel coma coefficient; wavefront maps

kerf.note: Coma aberration metrics (OPTICS-COMA-COMPUTE): pupil-rim ray bundle trace at N azimuths (Welford 1986 §11.4 / Born & Wolf §5.3); comatic flare length = mean Y-displacement of tangential-fan rays from chief-ray image point; sagittal coma = tangential/3 (Welford §11.4 eq. 11.4.4); total coma = sqrt(tan²+sag²); Seidel S_II prediction = 3x|S_II|x|y_chief| (Born & Wolf §5.3 eq. 5.3.29); depth bar: BK7 biconvex at 14 deg coma > 1 um; Seidel match < 0.1% at 5 deg; LLM tool optics_compute_coma; 33 hermetic tests. PLUS: Seidel coma coefficient S_II (OPTICS-SEIDEL-COMA): per-surface third-order Seidel coma sum S_II = sum(-A_j*Abar_j*h_j*delta(u/n)_j) via dual paraxial-ray trace (Welford 1986 §7 eq. 7.42 / Born & Wolf §5.3); SeidelComaReport: S_II, coma_waves_at_lambda (|3*S_II*y_chief|/(8*lambda)), dominant_surface_idx, per_surface_contributions; BK7 biconvex on-axis -> coma_waves=0; off-axis 5 deg -> S_II nonzero; aplanatic BK7+SF2 doublet -> |S_II| reduced; LLM tool optics_compute_seidel_coma; 34 hermetic tests. Honest: paraxial Seidel only; no chromatic coma; no defocus residual; stop at first surface; no higher-order coma. Ref: Welford (1986) §7 eq. 7.42; Born & Wolf (1999) §5.3 eq. 5.3.29.

features.feature: Petzval field curvature (1/R_P, flat-field condition, per-surface breakdown)

competitor.note: Petzval field curvature and field-flatness analysis; Petzval radius vs field angle; flat-field lens design tools; astigmatic field curve plots

kerf.note: Petzval field curvature (OPTICS-PETZVAL-CURVATURE-COMPUTE): P = Σ (n_after−n_before)/(n_before·n_after·R_i) (Hecht §6.3.2 / Born & Wolf §4.5); R_P = 1/P; flat-field score 1/(1+|P|·100); BK7 biconvex oracle (R1=+50, R2=−50, n=1.5168): P ≈ 0.01363 mm⁻¹, R_P ≈ 73.4 mm (within 1%); flat-field condition Σφ/n=0 → P=0 verified; plano surfaces (|R|=1e18) contribute 0; stop-shift invariance; per-surface breakdown with is_plano flag; LLM tool optics_compute_petzval_curvature; 26 hermetic tests. Honest: paraxial (third-order) only; astigmatism (S_III) not included; P=0 flat Petzval sphere does NOT guarantee zero field curvature in presence of astigmatism (Hecht §6.3.2); thick-lens pupil-shift corrections not modelled.

competitor.note: Full polychromatic analysis: longitudinal and transverse chromatic aberration; Sellmeier dispersion for all catalog glasses; achromatic doublet design

kerf.note: Longitudinal chromatic aberration (OPTICS-CHROMATIC-FOCUS): Sellmeier equation n²(λ)=1+Σ B_i λ²/(λ²−C_i) for BK7/F2/SF6/K5/SF11/BK10 (Schott catalog 2023); per-wavelength BFL via ABCD thin-lens stack; LCA_FC=BFL(486nm)−BFL(656nm); Abbe V-number (singlet); BK7 V≈64.2 verified; achromat BK7+F2 LCA<0.1mm verified; SF6 high LCA verified; LLM tool optics_compute_chromatic_focus; 34 hermetic tests. Honest: paraxial thin-lens LCA only; chromatic lateral aberration (transverse colour) not computed.

features.feature: Abbe number (V-number) and partial dispersion from Sellmeier coefficients

competitor.note: Glass map browser: V_d vs n_d for all catalog glasses; partial dispersion plots; apochromat glass selection

kerf.note: Abbe number + partial dispersion (OPTICS-ABBE-NUMBER-COMPUTE): V_d = (n_d − 1)/(n_F − n_C) from Sellmeier Fraunhofer lines d/F/C (Hecht §6.3 / ISO 10110); secondary spectrum partial dispersion P_{F,g} = (n_g − n_F)/(n_F − n_C) for g-line (435.84 nm); BK7 V_d=64.17, F2=36.37, SF6=25.43, K5=59.45, BK10=66.95 (all within 0.1% of Schott catalog); SF11=25.37 (catalog 25.76; 1.5% gap due to Sellmeier fit vs spectrophotometric measurement, documented in honest_flag per Schott TIE-29 §3.3); LLM tool optics_compute_abbe_number; 30 hermetic tests. Honest: catalog nominal/melt-mean Sellmeier coefficients; melt-to-melt V_d variation ±0.3–0.5% (Schott TIE-31).

competitor.note: Full relative illumination map per field; shows cos4 fall-off and physical vignetting contours

kerf.note: 2-D relative illumination map (OPTICS-RELATIVE-ILLUM-MAP): for each (x,y) sensor position computes field angle theta=arctan(r/EFL) and evaluates RI(theta) = surviving-ray fraction; ri_map (aperture-clipping model) + cos4_map (natural cos4(theta) baseline, Hecht §6.6 / Slyusarev §3.4); 1-D radial pre-computation exploits rotational symmetry; corner_ri + corner_cos4 diagnostics; BK7 biconvex @60mm half-sensor: theta_corner~60deg, cos4_corner~0.063; LLM tool optics_compute_relative_illum_map; 38 hermetic tests. Honest: monochromatic; rotationally-symmetric stack assumed; sensor tilt/telecentricity not modelled. Ref: Welford 1986 §4.5; Hecht §6.6; Slyusarev §3.4.

competitor.note: Entrance/exit pupil position, size, and chief-ray angle at pupil; pupil aberration analysis

kerf.note: Entrance pupil position and size (OPTICS-ENTRANCE-PUPIL-POS): paraxial reverse-trace of marginal ray from aperture stop through front sub-stack (Welford 1986 §4.4 / Hecht §6.6); position_z_mm from first surface vertex; D-element transverse magnification; converging front → real pupil; diverging front → virtual pupil; LLM tool optics_compute_entrance_pupil; 38 hermetic tests. Exit pupil position and size (OPTICS-EXIT-PUPIL-POS): two-ray forward trace (Welford §4.4 eq. 4.4.5); position_z_mm from last surface vertex; Ramsden disk oracle: z=31.25 mm, radius=1.25 mm, m=0.25 for 4× telescope (Hecht §6.6); Welford §4.4 virtual exit pupil oracle (BK7 biconvex, z≈−3.41 mm); LLM tool optics_compute_exit_pupil; 43 hermetic tests. Honest: paraxial only; stop modelled as thin plane.

competitor.note: Diffraction-limited MTF analysis: OTF/MTF/PTF for arbitrary pupil shapes; aberration-weighted MTF via wavefront integration; polychromatic MTF

kerf.note: Diffraction-limited MTF for circular aperture (OPTICS-MTF-DIFFRACTION-LIMITED): closed-form on-axis MTF(ν) = (2/π)[arccos(ν/ν₀) − (ν/ν₀)·√(1−(ν/ν₀)²)] for ν ≤ ν₀, 0 otherwise; cutoff ν₀ = 1/(λ·F#) (Goodman §6.4 eq. 6-49; Hecht 5e §11.3.3); oracle: λ=550nm, F/4 → ν₀=454.5 cyc/mm, MTF(0)=1.0, MTF(ν₀/2)≈0.391 — all validated to <0.1%; mtf_at_50_percent Nyquist metric; LLM tool optics_compute_diffraction_mtf; 36 hermetic tests. Honest: diffraction-limited upper bound only — no aberrations (Seidel S_I–S_V), no defocus, no sensor MTF, no polychromatic weighting (polychromatic MTF = ∫W(λ)·MTF(λ)dλ, not implemented), on-axis only, circular aperture only.

features.feature: Pixel aperture MTF + Nyquist limit (sensor MTF)

competitor.note: Zemax OpticStudio detector MTF: requires Detector Rectangle object + FFT; pixel sinc not exposed as a first-class analytic tool

kerf.note: Pixel aperture MTF (OPTICS-PIXEL-MTF): MTF_pixel(ν) = |sinc(π·a·ν)| where a = pitch·fill_factor (Boreman §3.4; Hecht §11.3); PixelSensorSpec(pixel_pitch_um, fill_factor=1.0) → PixelMtfReport(nyquist_freq_cyc_per_mm, mtf_curve, mtf_at_nyquist, mtf_at_50_percent_nyquist, honest_caveat); Nyquist limit ν_N = 1/(2·p); oracle p=1.5μm ff=1: ν_N=333.33 cyc/mm, MTF(ν_N)=2/π≈0.6366, MTF(0)=1.0 — all validated to <1e-6; combine_mtf_curves() for incoherent system cascade MTF_system = MTF_optical × MTF_pixel (Boreman §2.1); LLM tool optics_compute_pixel_mtf; 36 hermetic tests. Honest: pixel aperture sinc ONLY — NOT modelled: silicon carrier-diffusion MTF (Boreman §3.4); inter-pixel crosstalk; anti-aliasing filter MTF; Bayer CFA demosaicing; charge-transfer inefficiency; non-square pixels.

competitor.note: Depth of field analysis: DoF display on lens data + Image Simulation module; hyperfocal table; defocus pupil wavefront

kerf.note: Depth-of-field + hyperfocal distance (OPTICS-FOCAL-DISTANCE-DEPTH): LensFocusSpec(focal_length_mm, f_number, focus_distance_mm, circle_of_confusion_mm=0.03) -> DepthOfFieldReport; H = f^2/(N*c) + f; D_near = D*(H-f)/(H+D-2f); D_far = D*(H-f)/(H-D) [inf when D>=H]; DoF = D_far - D_near [inf when D>=H]; oracle 50mm f/2.8 @ 5m c=0.03mm: H~29812mm (~29.81m), near~4286mm, far~5993mm, DoF~1707mm — all validated <0.01%; at focus=H: D_near=H/2 exactly, D_far=inf; D>=H -> DoF infinite; larger f/# -> wider DoF; LLM tool optics_compute_depth_of_field; 25 hermetic tests. Default CoC 0.03mm = 35mm full-frame standard (Greenleaf S3). Honest: geometric thin-lens model ONLY — does NOT add diffraction-limited Airy-disk blur to geometric CoC; at small apertures (f/# ~> f/16 for visible light) Airy disk 1.22*lambda*N approaches 0.03mm CoC, so geometric DoF is slightly optimistic; for combined blur circle: c_eff = sqrt(c_geom^2 + c_airy^2). Ref: Hecht (2017) S6.4; Greenleaf (1950) S3; Kingslake (1978) S4.

competitor.note: Full pupil chief-ray angle and telecentricity check via exact ray tracing through sequential surfaces; object-space, image-space, and doubly-telecentric configurations; field dependence; export to report

kerf.note: Telecentricity check (OPTICS-TELECENTRICITY): compute_telecentricity(lens_system_dict, field_height_mm=10.0, focus_shift_mm=0.5) -> TelecentricityReport(chief_ray_angle_object_deg, chief_ray_angle_image_deg, object_telecentric, image_telecentric, both_telecentric, max_magnification_variation_pct, honest_caveat); paraxial chief-ray trace via Welford nu-form (n'u' = nu - hc(n'-n)); chief-ray angle solved by superposition: Ray A (H, 0) + alpha*Ray B (obj_dist, 1), alpha = -h_A_stop/h_B_stop forces h=0 at stop; object-space telecentric <-> |angle_obj| < 0.5 deg (machine-vision convention); image-space telecentric <-> |angle_img| < 0.5 deg; magnification variation across +/-focus_shift_mm relative to Lagrange-invariant reference magnification; oracle: single-lens (f=100mm, stop at front focal plane, 5m object) -> object_telecentric=True, image_telecentric=False; doubly-telecentric relay (two equal lenses, stop at shared focal plane) -> both_telecentric=True; LLM tool optics_compute_telecentricity; 25 hermetic tests. Honest: PARAXIAL (first-order) ONLY — aspheric terms do not affect first-order chief-ray angle; stop modelled as thin plane; infinite-conjugate approximated by large finite distance; magnification variation uses nu-form thin-lens paraxial formula. Ref: Welford (1986) S3, S4.4; Smith MOE S5.4; Hecht S6.6.

competitor.note: OpticStudio computes effective F/# from image-side NA for finite-conjugate systems; exact pupil-aberration-corrected working f-number via ray tracing

kerf.note: Working f-number (OPTICS-FNO-WORKING): FnoWorkingSpec(nominal_f_number, magnification) -> FnoWorkingReport(nominal_f_number, working_f_number, exposure_loss_stops, image_irradiance_factor, honest_caveat); thin-lens formula N_w = N*(1+|m|) (Hecht S6.4 / Smith MOE S4.5); m = -s_i/s_o (negative for real images); oracle f/4 at 1:1 macro (m=-1) -> N_w=8.0, loss=2.0 stops exactly; f/2.8 at 1:2 (m=-0.5) -> N_w=4.2, loss~1.17 stops; image irradiance factor = (N/N_w)^2 = 1/(1+|m|)^2; exposure_loss_stops = 2*log2(1+|m|); m=0 -> N_w=N, loss=0 (no penalty); LLM tool optics_compute_working_fno; 28 hermetic tests. Honest: thin-lens (symmetric-pupil) approximation ONLY — for asymmetric lenses (retrofocus, telephoto, macro with floating elements) pupil magnification p = D_exit/D_entrance != 1; exact formula N_w = (1/p)*N*(1+|m|/p) (Smith MOE S4.5) can differ by 0.5-1 stop for extreme retrofocus designs; pupil-position measurement requires aperture-stop trace not in scope. Ref: Hecht (2017) S6.4; Smith (2008) S4.5; Ray (2002) S2.

competitor.note: OpticStudio computes aperture stop diameter from system f/# and entrance-pupil position; exact marginal-ray-based clear-aperture clipping check via ray tracing

kerf.note: Iris diameter map (OPTICS-IRIS-DIAMETER-MAP): IrisMapSpec(lens_system_dict, target_f_number, target_efl_mm=None) -> IrisDiameterReport(iris_diameter_mm, effective_f_number, efl_mm, surface_clearance_check, clipped, honest_caveat); paraxial formula D = EFL/f# (Welford S3.4 / Smith MOE S6); EFL computed via canonical marginal-ray trace (h=1, u=0) or overridden via target_efl_mm; marginal ray (h=D/2 at stop, u=0) traced forward through surfaces after the stop and backward through reversed sub-system for surfaces before stop; per-surface clearance_ratio = CA_radius / |h| (>1 = clears; <1 = clips); clipping flag when h > 0.95 * CA_radius; oracle: EFL=50 mm, f/4 -> D=12.5 mm; f/1.4 fast lens -> D~35.7 mm; stop_surface_index configurable; LLM tool optics_compute_iris_diameter_map; 25 hermetic tests. Honest: paraxial (first-order) marginal-ray trace only — aspheric higher-order sag terms ignored; D=EFL/f# is infinity-conjugate formula (finite-conjugate requires working f-number N_w=N*(1+|m|), Smith MOE S4.5); clear apertures are caller-supplied; production design requires exact skew-ray analysis (Zemax / CODE V / Welford S5). Ref: Welford (1986) S3.4; Smith (2008) S6; Hecht (2017) S6.4.

kerf.kerf_note: Epic gap: STOP requires coupling kerf-fem (thermal/structural) deformation maps into the optical path as Zernike wavefront error inputs. Architecturally feasible (Kerf owns both FEM and optics modules) but multi-sprint integration work.

competitor.note: Zemax OpticStudio: Huygens PSF, FFT PSF, geometric PSF; polychromatic and coherent variants; pupil-weighted PSF integration

kerf.note: Airy-disk diffraction-limited PSF (OPTICS-DIFFRACTION-PSF): DiffractionPSFSpec(wavelength_nm, aperture_diameter_mm, focal_length_mm) -> DiffractionPSFReport; radial intensity I(r)=[2·J₁(x)/x]² where x=π·D·r/(λ·f) (Hecht §10.2 eq. 10.22; Born & Wolf §8.5.2 eq. 8.41); Airy disk radius r_Airy=1.22·λ·F# (first zero of J₁); Rayleigh resolution criterion Δr=r_Airy (Hecht §10.2.7); FWHM≈1.03·λ·F# (Hecht eq. 10.59); scipy.special.j1 with pure-Python series fallback; oracle λ=550nm, D=10mm, f=50mm (F#=5): r_Airy=3.355 μm, FWHM=2.833 μm, I(0)=1.0, I(r_Airy)≈0 validated to <1e-6; LLM tool optics_compute_diffraction_psf; 32 hermetic tests. HONEST: SCALAR DIFFRACTION ONLY (Kirchhoff/Fraunhofer) -- no vector/polarisation effects (Richards-Wolf high-NA integral, Born & Wolf §8.7); circular aperture only; aberration-free (Seidel/Zernike wavefront error not modelled); monochromatic only; on-axis paraxial (NA=D/(2f) << 1). Ref: Hecht (2017) §10.2; Born & Wolf (1999) §8.5; Goodman (2005) §4.4.

features.feature: Singlet lens glass volume and weight (cost / weight budget)

competitor.note: Zemax OpticStudio reports lens volume and mass via 3D CAD export or macro scripting; built-in catalog includes glass densities

kerf.note: Singlet lens glass volume and weight (OPTICS-LENS-VOLUME): SingletLensSpec(radius_R1_mm, radius_R2_mm, center_thickness_mm, clear_aperture_radius_mm, glass_density_g_cm3=2.51) -> LensVolumeReport(volume_mm3, weight_g, edge_thickness_mm, sag1_mm, sag2_mm, lens_form, honest_caveat); sag h=|R|-sqrt(|R|^2-r^2) (Smith MOE §13.3 eq. 13.6 / Mahajan §1.2); cap V_cap=pi*h^2*(3|R|-h)/3; lens V=pi*r^2*ct +/- caps (Cartesian sign: convex subtracts, concave adds); oracle BK7 plano-convex R1=+100mm, R2=inf, ct=5mm, CA_r=12.5mm -> sag1=0.7843mm, V=2261.6mm^3, weight=5.677g verified analytically; lens_form in {biconvex, biconcave, plano_convex, plano_concave, meniscus, plano_plano}; BK7 density default 2.51 g/cm^3; LLM tool optics_compute_lens_volume; 35 hermetic tests. HONEST: SPHERICAL SURFACES ONLY -- aspheric sag h=Rc*r^2/(1+sqrt(1-(1+k)*c^2*r^2))+SUM(A_i*r^(2i)) requires numerical integration (out of scope); no AR coating mass (100-500 nm, negligible); homogeneous density (GRIN/melt-gradient differs <= 0.3%, Schott TIE-31); clear_aperture_radius != physical blank radius -- blank larger, weight higher. Ref: Smith (2008) §13.3; Mahajan (2011) §1.2; Schott TIE-31 (2023). | Paraxial chief-ray height trace (OPTICS-CHIEF-RAY-HEIGHT): trace_chief_ray(lens_system_dict, field_angle_deg, stop_surface_idx) -> ChiefRayHeightReport(per_surface_heights, image_height_mm, magnification, stop_surface_idx, chief_ray_at_stop_mm, object_angle_deg, image_angle_deg, honest_caveat); chief ray (principal ray from off-axis object through stop centre h_stop=0) traced via paraxial superposition nu-form (Welford §3 / Mahajan §2); oracle f=50mm thin lens theta=5deg -> image_height=f*tan(5deg)=4.374mm verified <1e-3mm; h_stop=0 by construction; image-space telecentric condition (stop at front focal plane -> u_img=0) verified; LLM tool optics_trace_chief_ray; 30 hermetic tests. HONEST: PARAXIAL ONLY; aperture stop position must be supplied; rotationally symmetric; image height = f*tan(theta) no distortion. Ref: Welford (1986) §3, §3.7; Mahajan (2011) §2; Smith (2008) §5.

competitor.note: Physical optics propagation (POP); Mueller/Jones matrix polarisation; diffraction grating analysis

kerf.note: Scalar physical-optics propagation (POP): angular-spectrum method (Goodman §3.10, exact near-field), Fresnel transfer-function (§4.2, paraxial), Fraunhofer far-field (§4.3, single FFT). Gaussian source, thin-lens quadratic phase, circular aperture. Three analytic oracles validated: (1) Gaussian w(z) < 1% error vs w0·√(1+(z/zR)²); (2) Airy first null < 0.1% error vs 1.22λf/D; (3) Parseval energy conservation < 0.1%. No polarisation (Jones/Mueller), no multi-element sequential-POP optimisation, no diffraction grating.
kerf.kerf_note: Remaining gaps: full sequential POP through a multi-element system with aperture stops + pupil sampling (Zemax POP workflow); Jones/Mueller polarisation matrices; diffraction grating analysis. These are follow-on epics.

features.feature: Aspheric surface sagitta z(r) + arrow chart (ISO 10110-12)

competitor.note: Aspheric surface editor with sag profile display and ISO 10110-12 export; supports conic, even asphere, Q-polynomial (Forbes), XY polynomial, and freeform (Zernike) surface types

kerf.note: Sagitta arrow chart (OPTICS-SAGITTA-ARROW-CHART): conic + even-power aspheric surface z(r) = c*r^2/(1+sqrt(1-(1+k)*c^2*r^2)) + sum(a_i*r^(2i+4)) sampled across clear aperture; SVG chart with radius/sagitta axes + slope arrow markers at every 5th sample (dz/dr); AsphericSurfaceSpec dataclass; SagittaArrowChartResult (sagitta_samples, max_sagitta_mm, conic_only_sagitta_mm, aspheric_contribution_mm, svg_chart, honest_caveat); sphere R=50 r=10 oracle z~1.0102 verified <1e-6; paraboloid k=-1 z=r^2/(2R) exact; hemisphere z=R at edge; domain-violation guard; LLM tool optics_compute_sagitta_arrow_chart; 28 hermetic tests. Honest: conic + even-power polynomial asphere ONLY (ISO 10110-12 S6.2); no Zernike / freeform / Q-polynomial / XY-polynomial / off-axis / tilted surfaces. Ref: Welford (1986) S3.3; ISO 10110-12:2019.

competitor.note: No LLM interface in Ansys Zemax OpticStudio as of May 2026

kerf.note: Chat-native: describe optical system in plain language; Kerf routes to ray trace backend

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels can classify rational quadratic NURBS as conics (degree-2 gate) but do not expose a general algebraic discriminant-based classifier for arbitrary-degree curves or raw point sets

kerf.note: detect_conic_type(curve_or_points, num_samples=100, residual_threshold_mm=0.01) -> ConicDetectResult(conic_type, conic_coefficients, eccentricity, rms_residual_mm, honest_caveat); fits general conic Ax²+Bxy+Cy²+Dx+Ey+F=0 via SVD null-space (Pratt 1987 + Fitzgibbon-Pilu-Fisher 1999); classifies by discriminant B²−4AC: circle|ellipse|parabola|hyperbola|line|free_form; eccentricity from 3×3 homogeneous matrix eigendecomposition; LLM tool nurbs_detect_conic_type. HONEST: algebraic fit is susceptible to noise bias — for production-quality circle detection use Pratt/Taubin (curve_circle_fit.py); parabola discrimination at discriminant boundary is fragile under noise.

features.name: Degree-5 NURBS curve fit with G2 curvature-continuous end conditions (P&T §9.4)

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels provide G1 (tangent) end-condition interpolation; G2 (curvature) endpoint matching for freeform curve fitting is less common and typically requires accessing lower-level knot/CP construction APIs

kerf.note: fit_curve_g2(spec: G2FitSpec, degree=5) -> G2FitResult; degree-5 B-spline fit to 3-D data points with G2 (curvature-continuous) end conditions; prescribe start_tangent_xyz, start_curvature_xyz, end_tangent_xyz, end_curvature_xyz; chord-length parameterisation + clamped averaging-rule knots + analytic endpoint CPs (P&T §9.4 Eqs 9.26-9.29); interior CPs by least-squares collocation; 22 hermetic tests (straight-line residual<1e-9, circle-arc residual<1e-6, G2 constraints<1e-9, G1 constraints<1e-9); LLM tool nurbs_fit_curve_g2. HONEST: chord-length only — centripetal parameterisation (Lee 1989) may give better results for high-aspect-ratio or irregularly-spaced data; curvature vectors are parametric C''(t), NOT Frenet kappa*n_hat — use adjacent curve's C''(t_join) for blending. Refs: Piegl & Tiller §9.4; Farin CAGD Ch 10.

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels can classify rational quadratic NURBS as conics (degree-2 gate) but do not expose a general algebraic discriminant-based classifier for arbitrary-degree curves or raw point sets

kerf.note: detect_conic_type(curve_or_points, num_samples=100, residual_threshold_mm=0.01) -> ConicDetectResult(conic_type, conic_coefficients, eccentricity, rms_residual_mm, honest_caveat); fits general conic Ax²+Bxy+Cy²+Dx+Ey+F=0 via SVD null-space (Pratt 1987 + Fitzgibbon-Pilu-Fisher 1999); classifies by discriminant B²−4AC: circle|ellipse|parabola|hyperbola|line|free_form; eccentricity from 3×3 homogeneous matrix eigendecomposition; LLM tool nurbs_detect_conic_type. HONEST: algebraic fit is susceptible to noise bias — for production-quality circle detection use Pratt/Taubin (curve_circle_fit.py); parabola discrimination at discriminant boundary is fragile under noise.

features.name: Degree-5 NURBS curve fit with G2 curvature-continuous end conditions (P&T §9.4)

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels provide G1 (tangent) end-condition interpolation; G2 (curvature) endpoint matching for freeform curve fitting is less common and typically requires accessing lower-level knot/CP construction APIs

kerf.note: fit_curve_g2(spec: G2FitSpec, degree=5) -> G2FitResult; degree-5 B-spline fit to 3-D data points with G2 (curvature-continuous) end conditions; prescribe start_tangent_xyz, start_curvature_xyz, end_tangent_xyz, end_curvature_xyz; chord-length parameterisation + clamped averaging-rule knots + analytic endpoint CPs (P&T §9.4 Eqs 9.26-9.29); interior CPs by least-squares collocation; 22 hermetic tests (straight-line residual<1e-9, circle-arc residual<1e-6, G2 constraints<1e-9, G1 constraints<1e-9); LLM tool nurbs_fit_curve_g2. HONEST: chord-length only — centripetal parameterisation (Lee 1989) may give better results for high-aspect-ratio or irregularly-spaced data; curvature vectors are parametric C''(t), NOT Frenet kappa*n_hat — use adjacent curve's C''(t_join) for blending. Refs: Piegl & Tiller §9.4; Farin CAGD Ch 10.

HONEST: residuals use bilinear baseline, NOT a closed-form NURBS fit.
