features.feature: SubD limit-surface mesh export to glTF 2.0

competitor.note: Rhino 8 can export mesh to glTF via third-party plug-ins; Blender exports SubD limit-surface to glTF natively

kerf.note: export_limit_to_gltf: CC limit-surface exported as glTF 2.0 — both .gltf (JSON + base64 buffer) and .glb (binary GLB container). Valid asset.version='2.0'; POSITION VEC3 FLOAT + SCALAR UNSIGNED_INT indices; quads fan-triangulated; cube cage 2 levels → 192 triangles; GLB magic b'glTF' + version 2; vertex/face counts round-trip via parse_gltf; sphere cage convergence. Honest (v1): geometry only — no materials, normals attribute, textures, animations, skinning; no Draco compression; pure-Python (json+struct). 39 tests. LLM tool subd_export_limit_to_gltf. Ref: Khronos glTF 2.0.

competitor.note: Rhino 8 can export SubD meshes as STEP via polyhedral B-rep; Catia/SolidWorks export subdivision surfaces to STEP as faceted solid

kerf.note: export_limit_to_step: CC limit-surface exported as STEP AP242 faceted B-rep (ISO 10303-242:2020 / ISO 10303-21). Valid STEP file with FILE_DESCRIPTION + FILE_NAME + FILE_SCHEMA + DATA section; CARTESIAN_POINT / VERTEX_POINT / EDGE_CURVE / EDGE_LOOP / FACE_OUTER_BOUND / ADVANCED_FACE on PLANE surfaces / OPEN_SHELL / SHELL_BASED_SURFACE_MODEL / SHAPE_REPRESENTATION; cube cage 2 levels → 96 ADVANCED_FACEs; face count scales 4× per level; sphere cage bounding-box convergence; structural validity verified (header + DATA + ENDSEC). Honest: flat PLANE per polygon (STEP has no native SubD primitive; not smooth NURBS). 38 tests. LLM tool subd_export_limit_to_step. Ref: ISO 10303-242:2020.

features.feature: SubD limit-surface curve along cage edges

competitor.note: Rhino 8 ExtractSubDEdge / SubDEdgeCurve extracts limit-surface curves from SubD cage edges

kerf.note: walk_along_cage_edges: CC limit-surface polyline along cage edge chain — boundary=cage, creased=crease curve, smooth=limit arc; intermediate points via index-tracked CC subdivision + vertex limit stencils; LLM tool subd_walk_edge_chain; 14 tests.

competitor.note: Rhino 8 Section / Contour commands intersect SubD (converted to mesh) with a plane; limit-surface exact plane intersection not available as a SubD-native API

kerf.note: walk_subd_limit_cross_plane: samples (u,v) grid on each cage face via Stam (1998) exact limit-position evaluator (bi-cubic B-spline for regular patches, eigenstructure for extraordinary vertices); sign-change detection + 5-iteration bisection refinement (error ≈ cell_size/32); CrossCurveResult with points, face_indices_crossed, num_intersections, honest_caveat; LLM tool subd_walk_limit_cross_plane. Honest: discrete sample walk — coarse grids miss sub-cell features; 5-iter bisection (not sub-pixel); boundary duplicates possible; unsorted output.

competitor.note: Rhino 8 Section command intersects NURBS surfaces with a plane; returns polyline or NURBS curve

kerf.note: compute_surface_cross_section: samples S(u,v) on (nu×nv) grid, computes signed distance d(u,v)=(S−P)·n̂, detects sign-change grid edges, refines each crossing via 5 bisection iterations (error ≈ cell_size/32), chains points into polyline components via greedy nearest-neighbour. SurfaceCrossSectionResult: intersection_points_3d, num_intersections, num_components, is_closed_loop, honest_caveat. LLM tool nurbs_compute_surface_cross_section. Re-exported from geom/__init__.py. 22 hermetic tests. Honest: sample-based — sub-cell features may be missed at low nu/nv; 5-iter bisection (not sub-pixel); component ordering greedy (may mis-order interleaved loops). Refs: Sederberg CAGD §7.3; Patrikalakis-Maekawa §5.

competitor.note: Symmetry command — mirror-plane symmetry on SubD; no scored multi-axis detection

kerf.note: PCA mirror planes (Podolak 2006) + n-fold rotational axes (Mitra-Guibas-Pauly 2006) + spherical flag; quality score 0–1; subd_detect_symmetry + subd_enforce_symmetry LLM tools

competitor.note: Maya Select Edge Ring, Blender Select Edge Ring (Alt+click) — follows opposite edges across each quad face for loop-cut / bevel / bridge operations

kerf.note: compute_edge_ring(cage, start_edge): traverses opposite edges across quad faces (Maya/Blender Bridge ring pattern); closed on cube (4-ring) and torus; open at mesh boundary; is_degenerate flag at non-quad faces; LLM tool subd_compute_edge_ring; 25 tests.

competitor.note: Maya Select Edge Loop (Alt+click), Blender Alt+click edge loop — walks opposite edges of adjacent quads; terminates at irregular vertex (valence≠4) or mesh boundary; basis for loop-cut, bevel, quad-strip extraction

kerf.note: select_edge_loop(cage_mesh, start_edge_idx): traverses opposite edges of each quad face (skip-2 rule, Bommes-Lévy-Pietroni 2013 §3.2); terminates at irregular vertex (valence≠4), boundary, or non-quad face; EdgeLoopResult with edge_indices, vertex_indices (flat), closed, terminated_at_irregular, irregular_vertex_valences, honest_caveat; oracles: 4×4 torus closed length 4; 6×8 torus lengths {6,8}; cube (valence-3) terminates; cylinder axial-interior edge closes length nu; LLM tool subd_select_edge_loop; quads only (no tris); manifold assumed; 25 tests.

features.feature: SubD cage face-loop selection for ring-cut planning (Bommes-Lévy-Pietroni §3.2)

competitor.note: Maya Select Face Loop / Blender face loop selection — walks faces by hopping through opposite-edge adjacent quads; used for ring-cut, loop-cut target, subdivision sweep selection; terminates at non-quad face or mesh boundary

kerf.note: select_face_loop(cage_mesh, start_face_idx, walk_direction=0): hops through opposite-edge adjacent quads; walk_direction 0/1 selects which opposite-edge pair to cross, giving orthogonal rings; FaceLoopResult with face_indices, closed, terminated_at_irregular, irregular_face_indices, honest_caveat; oracles: 4×4 torus dir=0 closed ring 4; 4×6 torus dir=0→length 4 dir=1→length 6; cylinder (nu=12) dir=1 → closed ring 12; cube → closed 4-ring; mixed tri/quad → terminated; non-quad start → immediate termination; LLM tool subd_select_face_loop; HONEST: quads only — non-quad face terminates walk immediately; boundary edges terminate; walk_direction relative to start face winding; manifold assumed; 20 tests.

competitor.note: Maya polySplit / Blender subdivide_edges on one edge; not exposed as a programmatic API with topology-count guarantees.

kerf.note: subdivide_edge(cage, edge_index, position_t=0.5): inserts midpoint vertex on a single cage edge (not a full CC pass); each adjacent face split into triangle + quad; cube oracle V=8→9, F=6→8, E=12→15, Euler=2 ✓; boundary edge (1 adjacent face) and interior edge (2) both handled; invalid edge_index → ValueError; has_non_quad_input honest flag for non-quad input; LLM tool subd_subdivide_edge; 24 oracle-asserted tests. Ref: Catmull & Clark 1978; Stam 1998 §4; Maya polySplit.

competitor.note: Maya polyChamfer/merge vertex / Blender Merge by Distance / collapse edge — midpoint or target-point collapse; Rhino SubD CollapseEdge command; none expose QEM cost as programmatic API.

kerf.note: collapse_edge(cage, edge_idx): v_m = (v_a + v_b) / 2; degenerate faces (contained both endpoints) removed; vertex list compacted (net -1 vertex); midpoint position oracle; all face indices valid post-collapse; LLM tool subd_collapse_edge. HONEST: midpoint-only — no quadric error metric (Garland-Heckbert 1997); shape not preserved optimally; crease annotations on incident edges not preserved.

features.feature: SubD cage vertex merge (N-vertex centroid decimation)

competitor.note: Blender 'Merge by Distance' / Maya mergeVertex — merge multiple selected vertices to average (centroid) or target position; no programmatic API with topology-count guarantees; no QEM-optimal placement.

kerf.note: merge_vertices(cage, vertex_indices): v_c = centroid of named vertices; all face refs to any named vertex rewritten to v_c; faces containing ≥ 2 named vertices become degenerate and are removed; vertex list compacted (net -(|S|-1) vertices); empty/single-index list → no-op; duplicates deduplicated; non-adjacent merge → no faces removed, vertex count drops by |S|-1; out-of-range index → ValueError; VertexMergeResult.merged_index valid; LLM tool subd_merge_vertices; 20 oracle-asserted tests. HONEST: centroid placement only — no quadric error metric (Garland-Heckbert 1997 §3); simple centroid; CC limit surface shape changes after merge; crease annotations on incident edges not preserved.

features.feature: SubD cage dual mesh (face-centroid dual vertices + CCW vertex-star dual faces)

competitor.note: Neither Fusion 360 nor Blender/Maya expose a programmatic dual-mesh API for SubD cages with boundary-handling guarantees and honest caveats.

kerf.note: compute_dual_mesh(cage_mesh): one dual vertex per primal face (face centroid); one dual face per primal vertex (CCW-ordered ring of neighboring face centroids via atan2 angular sort on tangent plane); DualMeshResult(dual_vertices, dual_faces, num_irregular_dual_faces, honest_caveat); cube oracle: 6 dual verts + 8 triangular dual faces (combinatorial octahedron), 0 irregular; 4×4 torus (valence-4) → 16 quad dual faces, 0 irregular; plane 2×2 → boundary vertices flagged irregular; cylinder barrel → correct dual vertex/face count; LLM tool subd_compute_dual_mesh; 28 oracle-asserted tests. HONEST: closed manifold meshes only for complete dual faces — boundary primal edges produce incomplete (open) dual faces; angular sort may mis-order concave vertex stars; dual vertices are face centroids only (not CC limit-surface points); non-manifold input produces topologically undefined dual faces.

features.feature: SubD cage edge flip (triangles only, topological)

competitor.note: Blender 'Rotate Edge' / Maya polySplit edge flip / Rhino SubD FlipEdge — topological flip of a shared edge between two adjacent triangles; none expose programmatic API with boundary/non-manifold/quad error guarantees or explicit no-Delaunay caveats.

kerf.note: flip_edge(cage_mesh, edge_idx): given edge (v_a,v_b) shared by exactly two triangles T1=(v_a,v_b,v_c) and T2=(v_a,v_b,v_d), replaces them with T1'=(v_a,v_c,v_d) and T2'=(v_b,v_d,v_c); vertex list unchanged; face count unchanged (2 in → 2 out); winding order of T1 preserved in both outputs; EdgeFlipResult(new_cage_faces, num_edges_flipped, flipped_edge_indices, honest_caveat); oracle: ABC+BCD share BC → flip gives {A,B,D}+{A,C,D} sharing AD ✓; double flip restores original topology ✓; boundary edge (1 face) → ValueError; non-manifold edge (>2 faces) → ValueError; quad/n-gon face → ValueError; LLM tool subd_flip_edge; 14 oracle-asserted tests. HONEST: TOPOLOGICAL FLIP ONLY — no Delaunay in-circle test; caller must test in-circle criterion if Delaunay quality is required; triangles only — quad faces raise ValueError; crease/UV/colour attributes on flipped edge not preserved.

competitor.note: Fusion 360 Design Advisor flags thin walls for injection moulding; Solidworks DFMXpress checks wall thickness; neither exposes programmatic per-face API.

kerf.note: check_shell_walls(body, process, material) verifies post-shell wall thickness against injection_molding (Menges 2001 §3.3 Table 3.3: t_min/t_max per material, flow-length correction), sheet_metal (Boothroyd-Dewhurst §5), and FDM/SLA/SLS/DLP/MJF nozzle-diameter rules; ShellWallReport with per-face FaceWallResult, violations_thin/thick; LLM tool brep_check_shell_walls; 20 oracle-asserted tests; honest caveat: static rule-based only, no CFD or mould-fill simulation.

kerf.note: compute_exact_surface_area: A = ∫∫ sqrt(EG-F²) du dv; 5×5 GL per knot-span cell + adaptive subdivision; SurfaceAreaReport; LLM tool nurbs_surface_area_exact (do Carmo §2.5 / Mortenson §10.4). v1: full UV domain only. ALSO: compute_face_area_exact (BREP-FACE-AREA-EXACT): exact area of a B-rep Face via first-fundamental-form; FaceAreaResult{area_mm2, num_quadrature_points, relative_error_estimate, honest_caveat}; handles NurbsSurface (analytic partials) + Plane/Cylinder/Sphere/Torus (FD partials); LLM tool brep_compute_face_area_exact; 34 hermetic tests. HONEST: trimmed faces use bounding-rectangle approximation if no trim curves provided (v1).

competitor.note: Rhino ExtendCurve command (Line/Arc/Smooth modes); G1 tangent via Natural extension

kerf.note: extend_edge_curve(curve, delta_length_mm, end, continuity) extends a NurbsCurve beyond its parametric domain by ΔL mm; G1 tangent-continuous join via cubic Bézier tangent extrapolation (Piegl & Tiller §10.4; Mortenson §3.7); colinear CPs give a straight extension segment; degree-1 originals raised to degree-3. LLM tool brep_extend_edge_curve. 21 hermetic tests. CAVEAT: G1 only — G2 curvature-continuous extension not yet implemented.

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels can classify rational quadratic NURBS as conics (degree-2 gate) but do not expose a general algebraic discriminant-based classifier for arbitrary-degree curves or raw point sets

kerf.note: detect_conic_type(curve_or_points, num_samples=100, residual_threshold_mm=0.01) -> ConicDetectResult(conic_type, conic_coefficients, eccentricity, rms_residual_mm, honest_caveat); fits general conic Ax²+Bxy+Cy²+Dx+Ey+F=0 via SVD null-space (Pratt 1987 + Fitzgibbon-Pilu-Fisher 1999); classifies by discriminant B²−4AC: circle|ellipse|parabola|hyperbola|line|free_form; eccentricity from 3×3 homogeneous matrix eigendecomposition; LLM tool nurbs_detect_conic_type. HONEST: algebraic fit is susceptible to noise bias — for production-quality circle detection use Pratt/Taubin (curve_circle_fit.py); parabola discrimination at discriminant boundary is fragile under noise.

features.name: Degree-5 NURBS curve fit with G2 curvature-continuous end conditions (P&T §9.4)

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels provide G1 (tangent) end-condition interpolation; G2 (curvature) endpoint matching for freeform curve fitting is less common and typically requires accessing lower-level knot/CP construction APIs

kerf.note: fit_curve_g2(spec: G2FitSpec, degree=5) -> G2FitResult; degree-5 B-spline fit to 3-D data points with G2 (curvature-continuous) end conditions; prescribe start_tangent_xyz, start_curvature_xyz, end_tangent_xyz, end_curvature_xyz; chord-length parameterisation + clamped averaging-rule knots + analytic endpoint CPs (P&T §9.4 Eqs 9.26-9.29); interior CPs by least-squares collocation; 22 hermetic tests (straight-line residual<1e-9, circle-arc residual<1e-6, G2 constraints<1e-9, G1 constraints<1e-9); LLM tool nurbs_fit_curve_g2. HONEST: chord-length only — centripetal parameterisation (Lee 1989) may give better results for high-aspect-ratio or irregularly-spaced data; curvature vectors are parametric C''(t), NOT Frenet kappa*n_hat — use adjacent curve's C''(t_join) for blending. Refs: Piegl & Tiller §9.4; Farin CAGD Ch 10.

features.feature: NURBS curve degree reduction (for STEP export compatibility)

competitor.note: Rhino: ChangeDegree command reduces degree; internal Forrest-Piegl algorithm; no explicit deviation report exposed to user

kerf.note: lower_curve_degree (Piegl-Tiller §6.5 Forrest-Piegl + Schumaker §6): reduces degree by 1 or to target; returns DegreeLowerResult with max_deviation_mm, mean_deviation_mm, exact flag and honest_caveat; exact (max_dev < 1e-9) when input was previously degree-elevated; Forrest-Piegl minimises local per-segment error (NOT global L2); deviation sampled at 33 pts/segment. LLM tool nurbs_lower_curve_degree. Primary use-case: STEP export to CATIA V4/V5 / legacy ACIS kernels that reject degree ≥ 5 curves.

features.feature: B-rep face normal flip detection and correction

competitor.note: No public API for automatic detection and correction of inverted face normals in MatrixGold / Rhino (manual via FlipNormal command only)

kerf.note: brep_detect_and_flip_face_normals: iterative neighbour-consensus BFS voting (Mantyla §6.4; Hoffmann §3). Detects inverted faces on CONVEX curved surfaces (adjacent outward normals co-oriented, dot>threshold). CAVEATS: (1) perpendicular-adjacent faces (e.g. unit cube) produce no detectable signal — cannot correct a cube from normals alone; (2) concave surfaces and open flat meshes are not supported; (3) isolated faces (no neighbours) unchanged; (4) absolute orientation seeded from first face per component. For production use OCCT BRepLib::OrientClosedSolid.

competitor.note: Most CAD kernels can classify rational quadratic NURBS as conics (degree-2 gate) but do not expose a general algebraic discriminant-based classifier for arbitrary-degree curves or raw point sets

HONEST: algebraic fit is susceptible to noise bias — for production-quality circle detection use Pratt/Taubin (curve_circle_fit.py); parabola discrimination at discriminant boundary is fragile under noise.

kerf.note: fit_curve_g2(spec: G2FitSpec, degree=5) -> G2FitResult; degree-5 B-spline fit to 3-D data points with G2 (curvature-continuous) end conditions; prescribe start_tangent_xyz, start_curvature_xyz, end_tangent_xyz, end_curvature_xyz; chord-length parameterisation + clamped averaging-rule knots + analytic endpoint CPs (P&T §9.4 Eqs 9.26-9.29); interior CPs by least-squares collocation; 22 hermetic tests (straight-line residual<1e-9, circle-arc residual<1e-6, G2 constraints<1e-9, G1 constraints<1e-9); LLM tool nurbs_fit_curve_g2. features.name: B-rep wire closed-loop + planarity pre-check (Mantyla §3; Hoffmann §4)

competitor.note: OCCT BRepCheck_Analyzer and BRep_Builder perform wire validity checks internally but do not expose a standalone ordered-edge-list closed-loop + planarity API with explicit max_endpoint_gap and SVD out-of-plane deviation fields for LLM/script interrogation

kerf.note: check_wire_closed(edges: list[EdgeSegment], tolerance_mm=1e-6) -> WireCheckReport; EdgeSegment dataclass: start_xyz, end_xyz, edge_id; WireCheckReport: closed (bool), planar (bool), max_endpoint_gap_mm, num_edges, plane_normal_xyz (SVD least-singular-vector), max_out_of_plane_deviation_mm, honest_caveat; closed = every consecutive pair connected AND last.end ≈ first.start within tolerance; planar = SVD best-fit plane over all endpoints (Pratt 1987; Eberly §6.6) has max_oop < tolerance; LLM tool brep_check_wire_closed registered (gated import); re-exported from geom/__init__.py; 26 hermetic tests (closed square closed=True/planar=True/normal=(0,0,1), open wire closed=False/gap>0, 3-D zig-zag planar=False, triangle closed+planar, hexagon, XZ-plane triangle normal=(0,1,0), large square, lifted vertex non-planar, tetrahedron-outline non-planar, tolerance sensitivity, empty ValueError). HONEST: edge order-dependent — scrambled/unordered edge lists are NOT automatically sorted; pass pre-ordered edges only; planarity is endpoint-only, curve interiors NOT sampled. Refs: Mantyla 'Solid Modeling' §3; Hoffmann §4.
